binary directional classification (**UP / DOWN**)\n    14→\n    15→- **Forecast horizon:** **15 minutes**\n    16→\n    17→- **Trading style:** high-frequency directional decision making\n    18→\n    19→- **Primary success metric:** **directional accuracy >= 65%**\n    20→\n    21→- **Secondary success metric:** **more than 90 bets per month**\n    22→\n    23→The system must optimize for both:\n    24→\n    25→1. **Prediction quality**: at least **65% directional accuracy**\n    26→\n    27→2. **Trading activity**: at least **90 valid bets per month**\n    28→\n    29→A model that is accurate but generates too few trades is not acceptable.  \n    30→\n    31→A model that generates many trades but fails to reach 65% directional accuracy is also not acceptable.\n    32→\n    33→---\n    34→\n    35→## Initial State (build first)\n    36→\n    37→S₀ = online continuous adaptive trading system which:\n    38→\n    39→- uses **online market data** for training and updating\n    40→\n    41→- uses **evolutionary model search** (mutation, crossover, replacement of weak models)\n    42→\n    43→- uses **strict walk-forward validation**\n    44→\n    45→- uses **online Sharpe filtering**\n    46→\n    47→- uses **horizon ensembles** H = {1h, 4h, 8h, 12h, 24h} as contextual signals\n    48→\n    49→- uses **consensus gating** for signal generation\n    50→\n    51→- uses **time-series foundation models** and sequence models\n    52→\n    53→- is centered on the **main execution target = BTC up/down over the next 15 minutes**\n    54→\n    55→---\n    56→\n    57→## Core Prediction Task\n    58→\n    59→At each decision point, the system must output one of:\n    60→\n    61→- **UP** = BTC is expected to be higher in 15 minutes\n    62→\n    63→- **DOWN** = BTC is expected to be lower in 15 minutes\n    64→\n    65→- optional: **NO TRADE** only if confidence is too low\n    66→\n    67→The system should be designed to maximize profitable directional decisions while maintaining enough signal frequency to exceed **90 bets per month**.\n    68→\n    69→---\n    70→\n    71→## Optimization Targets\n    72→\n    73→The system must optimize for the following target profile:\n    74→\n    75→- **Directional accuracy >= 65%**\n    76→\n    77→- **Bets per month > 90**\n    78→\n    79→- **Positive net profitability**\n    80→\n    81→- **Strong Sharpe ratio**\n    82→\n    83→- **Controlled drawdown**\n    84→\n    85→- **Robustness across market regimes**\n    86→\n    87→- **Stable out-of-sample performance**\n    88→\n    89→---\n    90→\n    91→## Fitness Priorities\n    92→\n    93→Fitness should prioritize:\n    94→\n    95→1. **Directional accuracy on 15m BTC prediction**\n    96→\n    97→2. **Monthly bet count**\n    98→\n    99→3. **PnL**\n   100→\n   101→4. **Sharpe ratio**\n   102→\n   103→5. **Drawdown control**\n   104→\n   105→6. **Regime robustness**\n   106→\n   107→7. **Consistency across walk-forward folds**\n   108→\n   109→Any candidate that fails either:\n   110→\n   111→- **accuracy < 65%**\n   112→\n   113→- **bets/month <= 90**\n   114→\n   115→should be considered below target even if other metrics look good.\n   116→\n   117→---\n   118→\n   119→## Data and Modeling Requirements\n   120→\n   121→The system should:\n   122→\n   123→- learn from **BTC market structure**\n   124→\n   125→- exploit **short-term directional patterns**\n   126→\n   127→- use **multi-timescale context** while keeping the target fixed at 15m\n   128→\n   129→- adapt online as conditions change\n   130→\n   131→- discover alpha from:\n   132→\n   133→  - price action\n   134→\n   135→  - volatility\n   136→\n   137→  - momentum\n   138→\n   139→  - order-flow proxies\n   140→\n   141→  - volume\n   142→\n   143→  - derivatives context if available\n   144→\n   145→  - regime features\n   146→\n   147→  - cross-horizon agreement\n   148→\n   149→Longer horizons {1h, 4h, 8h, 12h, 24h} should be used as **contextual ensemble inputs**, not as replacements for the core 15m target.\n   150→\n   151→---\n   152→\n   153→## Validation Rules\n   154→\n   155→The system must use:\n   156→\n   157→- strict **temporal splits**\n   158→\n   159→- strict **walk-forward validation**\n   160→\n   161→- no lookahead bias\n   162→\n   163→- no leakage\n   164→\n   165→- no random split for final evaluation\n   166→\n   167→- evaluation on realistic trading frequency\n   168→\n   169→- explicit measurement of:\n   170→\n   171→  - accuracy\n   172→\n   173→  - number of bets\n   174→\n   175→  - PnL\n   176→\n   177→  - Sharpe\n   178→\n   179→  - drawdown\n   180→\n   181→  - regime-specific performance\n   182→\n   183→A candidate is only truly good if it remains strong in out-of-sample walk-forward testing.\n   184→\n   185→---\n   186→\n   187→## Evolution Strategy\n   188→\n   189→Run this loop continuously:\n   190→\n   191→loop t = 1..∞\n   192→\n   193→    S_t = design_or_modify(S_{t-1})   # implement the current design\n   194→\n   195→    O_t = run(S_t)                    # train, validate, and trade / paper trade\n   196→\n   197→    P_t = measure(O_t)                # evaluate accuracy, bets/month, Sharpe, PnL, drawdown, regime behavior\n   198→\n   199→    Δ_t = reflect(S_t, P_t)           # identify weaknesses and bottlenecks\n   200→\n   201→    S_{t+1} = improve(S_t, Δ_t)       # produce a better design\n   202→\n   203→end\n   204→\n   205→---\n   206→\n   207→## Reflection Requirements\n   208→\n   209→During reflection, explicitly diagnose:\n   210→\n   211→- why accuracy is below 65% if target is missed\n   212→\n   213→- why signal frequency is below 90 bets/month if target is missed\n   214→\n   215→- whether the consensus gate is too strict\n   216→\n   217→- whether the model is overfitting to specific regimes\n   218→\n   219→- whether the ensemble is improving or diluting edge\n   220→\n   221→- whether the system is sacrificing too much frequency for precision\n   222→\n   223→- whether the system is producing too many low-quality trades\n   224→\n   225→- whether thresholding should be tightened or loosened\n   226→\n   227→The next design must directly address the identified weakness.\n   228→\n   229→---\n   230→\n   231→## Design Guidance\n   232→\n   233→The system should search for the best balance the below program to evolve a system S that discovers profitable trading strategies for BTC directional prediction on a 15-minute horizon.\n\n## Primary Objective\n\nBuild and continuously improve a trading system that predicts whether **BTC will go UP or DOWN over the next 15 minutes**.\n\nThe task is strictly:\n\n- **Asset:** BTC\n\n- **Prediction type:** binary directional classification (**UP / DOWN**)\n\n- **Forecast horizon:** **15 minutes**\n\n- **Trading style:** high-frequency directional decision making\n\n- **Primary success metric:** **directional accuracy >= 65%**\n\n- **Secondary success metric:** **more than 90 bets per month**\n\nThe system must optimize for both:\n\n1. **Prediction quality**: at least **65% directional accuracy**\n\n2. **Trading activity**: at least **90 valid bets per month**\n\nA model that is accurate but generates too few trades is not acceptable.  \n\nA model that generates many trades but fails to reach 65% directional accuracy is also not acceptable.\n\n---\n\n## Initial State (build first)\n\nS₀ = online continuous adaptive trading system which:\n\n- uses **online market data** for training and updating\n\n- uses **evolutionary model search** (mutation, crossover, replacement of weak models)\n\n- uses **strict walk-forward validation**\n\n- uses **online Sharpe filtering**\n\n- uses **horizon ensembles** H = {1h, 4h, 8h, 12h, 24h} as contextual signals\n\n- uses **consensus gating** for signal generation\n\n- uses **time-series foundation models** and sequence models\n\n- is centered on the **main execution target = BTC up/down over the next 15 minutes**\n\n---\n\n## Core Prediction Task\n\nAt each decision point, the system must output one of:\n\n- **UP** = BTC is expected to be higher in 15 minutes\n\n- **DOWN** = BTC is expected to be lower in 15 minutes\n\n- optional: **NO TRADE** only if confidence is too low\n\nThe system should be designed to maximize profitable directional decisions while maintaining enough signal frequency to exceed **90 bets per month**.\n\n---\n\n## Optimization Targets\n\nThe system must optimize for the following target profile:\n\n- **Directional accuracy >= 65%**\n\n- **Bets per month > 90**\n\n- **Positive net profitability**\n\n- **Strong Sharpe ratio**\n\n- **Controlled drawdown**\n\n- **Robustness across market regimes**\n\n- **Stable out-of-sample performance**\n\n---\n\n## Fitness Priorities\n\nFitness should prioritize:\n\n1. **Directional accuracy on 15m BTC prediction**\n\n2. **Monthly bet count**\n\n3. **PnL**\n\n4. **Sharpe ratio**\n\n5. **Drawdown control**\n\n6. **Regime robustness**\n\n7. **Consistency across walk-forward folds**\n\nAny candidate that fails either:\n\n- **accuracy < 65%**\n\n- **bets/month <= 90**\n\nshould be considered below target even if other metrics look good.\n\n---\n\n## Data and Modeling Requirements\n\nThe system should:\n\n- learn from **BTC market structure**\n\n- exploit **short-term directional patterns**\n\n- use **multi-timescale context** while keeping the target fixed at 15m\n\n- adapt online as conditions change\n\n- discover alpha from:\n\n  - price action\n\n  - volatility\n\n  - momentum\n\n  - order-flow proxies\n\n  - volume\n\n  - derivatives context if available\n\n  - regime features\n\n  - cross-horizon agreement\n\nLonger horizons {1h, 4h, 8h, 12h, 24h} should be used as **contextual ensemble inputs**, not as replacements for the core 15m target.\n\n---\n\n## Validation Rules\n\nThe system must use:\n\n- strict **temporal splits**\n\n- strict **walk-forward validation**\n\n- no lookahead bias\n\n- no leakage\n\n- no random split for final evaluation\n\n- evaluation on realistic trading frequency\n\n- explicit measurement of:\n\n  - accuracy\n\n  - number of bets\n\n  - PnL\n\n  - Sharpe\n\n  - drawdown\n\n  - regime-specific performance\n\nA candidate is only truly good if it remains strong in out-of-sample walk-forward testing.\n\n---\n\n## Evolution Strategy\n\nRun this loop continuously:\n\nloop t = 1..∞\n\n    S_t = design_or_modify(S_{t-1})   # implement the current design\n\n    O_t = run(S_t) identify weaknesses and bottlenecks\n\n    S_{t+1} = improve(S_t, Δ_t)       # produce a better design\n\nend\n\n---\n\n## Reflection Requirements\n\nDuring reflection, explicitly diagnose:\n\n- why accuracy is below 65% if target is missed\n\n- why signal frequency is below 90 bets/month if target is missed\n\n- whether the consensus gate is too strict\n\n- whether the model is overfitting to specific regimes\n\n- whether the ensemble is improving or diluting edge\n\n- whether the system is sacrificing too much frequency for precision\n\n- whether the system is producing too many low-quality trades\n\n- whether thresholding should be tightened or loosened\n\nThe next design must directly address the identified weakness.\n\n---\n\n## Design Guidance\n\nThe system should search for the best balance between:\n\n- **high directional precision**\n\n- **high enough trade frequency**\n\n- **strong risk-adjusted return**\n\n- **robustness through time**\n\nThis is not a low-frequency swing system.  \n\nThis is not a generic forecasting system.  \n\nIt is a **BTC 15-minute directional trading system** that must be both:\n\n- **accurate enough** (>= 65%)\n\n- **active validation with a single temporal split.\\n   204→        Train on data before the validation period, test on the most recent validation period.\\n   205→        This provides an honest OOS performance estimate without leakage.\\n   206→        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   207→        print(\\\"Running walk-forward validation (single split)...\\\", flush=True)\\n   208→\\n   209→        df_sorted = 223→\\n   224→        # Limit training samples to most recent MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES (controls memory/time)\\n   225→        if len(train_df) > ModelConfig.MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES:\\n   226→            train_df \"\"\"\n   161→        print(f\"Evolving population (input_dim={X_train.shape[1]})...\", flush=True)\n   162→\n   163→        pop = Population(\n   164→                    pred = ind.model.predict(X_train, threshold=0.5)\n   183→                    accuracy = (pred == y_train).mean()\n   184→\n   185→                    # Simple fitness based on training accuracy (we'll validate on walk-forward later)\n   186→                    ind.fitness = accuracy\n   187→                    print(f\"      -> fitness={ind.fitness:.4f}\", flush=True)\n   188→                except Exception as e:\n   189→                    print(f\"      -> training error: {e}\", flush=True)\n   190→                    ind.fitness = -1000.0\n   191→\n   192→            # Evolve to next generation, but not after the final generation\n   193→            if gen < generations - 1:\n   194→                print(\"  Evolving to next generation...\", flush=True)\n   195→                pop.evolve()\n   196→\n   197→            best = pop.get_best(1)[0]\n   198→            print(f\"    Best fitness: {best.fitness:.4f} (model={best.model_type})\", flush=True)\n   199→\n   200→        return pop\n   201→\n   202→    def run_walk_forward(\n   203→        self,\n   204→        df: pd.DataFrame\n   205→    ) -> Dict[str, float]:\n   206→        \"\"\"\n   207→        Run walk-forward validation with a single temporal split.\n   208→        Train on data before the validation period, test on the most recent validation period.\n   209→        This provides an honest OOS performance estimate without leakage.\n   210→        \"\"\"\n   211→        print(\"Running walk-forward validation (single split)...\", flush=True)\n   212→\n   213→        df_sorted = df.sort_values(\"open_time\").reset_index(drop=True)\n   214→\n   215→        # Determine validation period length (e.g., 7 days)\n   216→        val_days = ValidationConfig.VALID_DAYS\n   217→        val_rows = int(val_days * 24 * 60 / 15)  # number of 15-min candles\n   218→\n   219→        total_rows = len(df_sorted)\n   220→        if total_rows < val_rows + 1000:\n   221→            print(\"Insufficient data for walk-forward split.\", flush=True)\n   222→            return {\"accuracy\": 0.0, \"bets_per_month\": 0.0, \"n_folds\": 0}\n   223→\n   224→        # Split: validation = most recent val_rows, training = older data\n   225→        valid_df = df_sorted.iloc[-val_rows:].copy()\n   226→        train_df = df_sorted.iloc[:-val_rows].copy()\n   227→\n   228→        # Limit training samples to most recent MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES (controls memory/time)\n   229→        if len(train_df) > ModelConfig.MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES:\n   230→            train_df = train_df.tail(ModelConfig.MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES).copy()\n   231→            print(f\"Limited training to {len(train_df)} most recent samples\", flush=True)\n   232→\n   233→        print(f\"Train: {len(train_df)} samples, Validation: {len(valid_df)} samples\", flush=True)\n   234→\n   235→        X_train = train_df[self.feature_names]\n   236→        y_train = train_df[\"target\"]\n   237→        X_valid = valid_df[self.feature_names]\n   238→        y_valid = valid_df[\"target\"]\n   239→\n   240→        # Evolve a fresh population on the training set\n   241→        pop = Population(\n   242→            population_size=ModelConfig.POPULATION_SIZE,\n   243→            model_types=ModelConfig.MODEL_TYPES,\n   244→            mutation_rate=ModelConfig.MUTATION_RATE,\n   245→            crossover_rate=ModelConfig.CROSSOVER_RATE,\n   246→            elitism_count=ModelConfig.ELITISM_COUNT\n   247→        )\n   248→        pop.initialize(input_dim=X_train.shape[1], feature_names=self.feature_names)\n   249→\n   250→        for gen in range(ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION):\n   251→            print(f\"  Generation {gen+1}/{ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION}\", flush=True)\n   252→            # Evaluate individuals on training data\n   253→            for ind in pop.individuals:\n   254→                try:\n   255→                    ind.model.fit(X_train, y_train)\n   256→                    # Use training accuracy as fitness for selection\n   257→                    pred = ind.model.predict(X_train, threshold=0.5)\n   258→                    ind.fitness = (pred == y_train).mean()\n   259→                except Exception as e:\n   260→                    print(f\"    Training error: {e}\", flush=True)\n   261→                    ind.fitness = -1000.0\n   262→\n   263→            pop.evolve()\n   264→            best = pop.get_best(1)[0]\n   265→            print(f\"    Best fitness: {best.fitness:.4f} ({best.model_type})\", flush=True)\n   266→\n   267→        # Prepare close prices for simulation (used later and here)\n   268→        close_prices = valid_df[\"close\"]\n   269→\n   270→        # Re-score individuals on validation set using Sharpe (profitability)\n   271→        print(\"Re-scoring population on validation set (Sharpe-based)...\", flush=True)\n   272→        for i, ind in enumerate(pop.individuals):\n   273→            # Skip individuals that failed training\n   274→            if ind.fitness is None or ind.fitness <= -1000:\n   275→                print(f\"  Skipping individual {i+1}/{len(pop.individuals)} (failed training)\", flush=True)\n   276→                continue\n   277→            try:\n   278→                print(f\"  Rescoring individual {i+1}/{len(pop.individuals)} ({ind.model_type})\", flush=True)\n   279→                # Predict on validation\n   280→                pred_valid = ind.model.predict(X_valid, threshold=0.5)\n   281→                # Ensure pred_valid is a Series with index\n   282→                if isinstance(pred_valid, np.ndarray):\n   283→                    pred_valid = pd.Series(pred_valid, index=X_valid.index)\n   284→                # Create signals series aligned to close_prices (fill missing with -1)\n   285→                signals_ind = pred_valid.reindex(close_prices.index).fillna(-1).astype(int)\n   286→                # Run simulation\n   287→                self.trader.run(signals_ind, close_prices)\n   288→                v_metrics = self.trader.metrics\n   289→                # Use Sharpe as fitness (higher is better)\n   290→                ind.fitness = v_metrics.get('sharpe', -1000)\n   291→                print(f\"    -> Sharpe: {ind.fitness:.4f}\", flush=True)\n   292→                # Preserve validation metrics for later analysis if needed\n   293→                ind.validation_metrics = v_metrics\n   294→            except Exception as e:\n   295→                print(f\"  Validation rescoring error: {e}\", flush=True)\n   296→                ind.fitness = -1000.0\n   297→\n   298→        # Get top diverse models based on validation Sharpe\n   299→        top_models = pop.get_diverse_best(n=5)\n   300→\n   301→        # Generate consensus signals on validation set\n   302→        consensus = ConsensusGate(\n   303→            min_models_agree=ConsensusConfig.MIN_MODELS_AGREE,\n   304→            min_confidence=ConsensusConfig.MIN_CONFIDENCE\n   305→        )\n   306→        signals = consensus.decide(top_models, X_valid)\n   307→\n   308→        # Align close prices (indices correspond)\n   309→        close_prices = valid_df[\"close\"]\n   310→\n   311→        # Run simulator on signals\n   312→        self.trader.run(signals, close_prices)\n   313→        metrics = self.trader.metrics.copy()\n   314→\n   315→        # Compute raw accuracy\n   316→        mask = (signals != -1) & y_valid.notna()\n   317→        if mask.any():\n   318→            metrics[\"accuracy\"] = (signals[mask] == y_valid[mask]).mean()\n   319→        else:\n   320→            metrics[\"accuracy\"] = 0.0\n   321→\n   322→        metrics[\"n_folds\"] = 1\n   323→        print(f\"Walk-forward results: accuracy={metrics['accuracy']:.4f}, bets/month={metrics.get('bets_per_month', 0):.2f}, Sharpe={metrics.get('sharpe', 0):.4f}\", flush=True)\n   324→\n   325→        return metrics\n   326→\n   327→    def train_best_models(\n   328→        self,\n   329→        df: pd.DataFrame,\n   330→        population: Population,\n   331→        horizon_data: dict = None\n   332→    ):\n   333→        \"\"\"\n   334→        Train the best models on full recent data and optionally train 347→        # Get top models\n   348→        top_models = population.get_diverse_best(n=ConsensusConfig.MIN_MODELS_AGREE + 2)\n   349→        print(f\"Selected {len(top_models)} top models for 388→        self.top_models = top_models\n   389→\n   390→    def generate_current_signals(\n   391→        self,\n   392→        current_features: pd.DataFrame,\n   393→        use_horizon: bool = True\n   394→    ) -> pd.Series:\n   395→        \"\"\"\n   396→        Generate trading signals for the most recent data points.\n   397→        \"\"\"\n   398→        if self.top_models is None:\n   399→            raise RuntimeError(\"Models not trained. Call train_best_models first.\")\n   400→\n   401→        signals = self.consensus_gate.decide(self.top_models, current_features)\n   402→        return signals\n   403→\n   404→    def run_online_step(self, new_data: pd.DataFrame = None):\n   405→        \"\"\"\n   406→        Execute one iteration of the online evolution loop.\n   407→\n   408→        This is the main step method called repeatedly by Arbos.\n   409→        \"\"\"\n   410→        print(\"=\" * 60, flush=True)\n   411→        print(\"RUNNING ONLINE STEP\", flush=True)\n   412→        print(\"=\" * 60, flush=True)\n   413→\n   414→        # 1. Load/fetch latest data\n   415→        if new_data is None:\n   416→            df = self.load_or_fetch_data()\n   417→        else:\n   418→            df = new_data\n   419→\n   420→        # 2. For online operation, limit to most recent N days to bound runtime\n   421→        if ModelConfig.ONLINE_DATA_DAYS is not None:\n   422→            cutoff = df[\"open_time\"].max() - timedelta(days=ModelConfig.ONLINE_DATA_DAYS)\n   423→            df = df[df[\"open_time\"] >= cutoff].copy()\n   424→            print(f\"Online mode: limited data to last {ModelConfig.ONLINE_DATA_DAYS} days ({len(df)} rows)\", flush=True)\n   425→\n   426→        # 3. import SimulatedTrader\nfrom .trading.metrics import evaluate_predictions\n\n\nclass TradingSystem:\n    \"\"\"\n    Main trading system class that orchestrates the full workflow.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        data_path: str = None,\n        results_dir: str = PathConfig.RESULTS_DIR,\n        log_dir: str = PathConfig.LOGS_DIR\n    ):\n        self.data_path = data_path or Path(PathConfig.DATA_DIR) / f\"{DataConfig.SYMBOL.lower()}_{DataConfig.INTERVAL}.csv\"\n        self.results_dir = Path(results_dir)\n        self.log_dir = Path(log_dir)\n\n        self.results_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n        self.log_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n        # Current state\n        self.df = None\n        self.feature_names = []\n        self.main_population = None\n        self.horizon_ensemble = None\n        self.consensus_gate = None\n        self.validator = None\n        self.trader = None\n\n        # Load or initialize\n        self._init_components()\n\n    def _init_components(self):\n        \"\"\"Initialize all system components.\"\"\"\n        self.validator = WalkForwardValidator(\n            train_days=ValidationConfig.TRAIN_DAYS,\n            valid_days=ValidationConfig.VALID_DAYS,\n            step_days=ValidationConfig.STEP_DAYS,\n            min_train_samples=ValidationConfig.MIN_TRAIN_SAMPLES\n        )\n\n        self.trader = SimulatedTrader(\n            initial_capital=TradingConfig.INITIAL_CAPITAL,\n            fee_rate=TradingConfig.FEE_RATE,\n            slippage=TradingConfig.SLIPPAGE,\n            bet_size=TradingConfig.POSITION_SIZE\n        )\n\n        self.horizon_ensemble = HorizonEnsemble(horizons=FeatureConfig.HORIZONS)\n        self.consensus_gate = ConsensusGate(\n            min_models_agree=ConsensusConfig.MIN_MODELS_AGREE,\n            min_confidence=ConsensusConfig.MIN_CONFIDENCE\n        )\n\n    def load_or_fetch_data(self, force_refetch: bool = False) -> pd.DataFrame:\n        \"\"\"\n        Load data from disk or fetch from Binance if not available/forced.\n        \"\"\"\n        if not force_refetch and self.data_path.exists():\n            print(f\"Loading data from {self.data_path}\")\n            df = pd.read_csv(self.data_path, parse_dates=[\"open_time\"])\n        else:\n            print(\"Fetching fresh data from Binance...\")\n            df = fetch_and_save_historical(\n                symbol=DataConfig.SYMBOL,\n                interval=DataConfig.INTERVAL,\n                start_date=DataConfig.START_DATE,\n                filepath=str(self.data_path)\n            )\n\n        if df.empty:\n            raise ValueError(\"No data available\")\n\n        print(f\"Loaded {len(df)} rows of data\", flush=True)\n        print(f\"Date range: {df['open_time'].iloc[0]} to {df['open_time'].iloc[-1]}\", flush=True)\n        return df\n\n    def prepare_features(self, df: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:\n        \"\"\"\n        Prepare feature matrix X and target y.\n        \"\"\"\n        print(\"Preparing features...\", flush=True)\n\n        # Clean data\n        df = clean_data(df)\n\n        # Create target (next 15-minute direction)\n        df = create_target(df, forward_periods=1)\n\n        # Generate horizon ensemble features (these will be added as extra columns)\n        print(\"  Generating horizon features...\", flush=True)\n        horizon_features = generate_horizon_features(df, FeatureConfig.HORIZONS)\n\n        # Merge horizon features into df\n        df = df.merge(horizon_features, on=\"open_time\", how=\"left\")\n\n        # Drop rows with NaN in target\n        df = df.dropna(subset=[\"target\"])\n\n        # Forward fill any remaining NaNs\n        df = df.ffill().dropna()\n\n        print(f\"Prepared {len(df)} samples with {len(df.columns)} columns\", flush=True)\n        self.feature_names = [\n            col for col in df.columns\n            if col not in [\n                \"open_time\", \"open\", \"high\", \"low\", \"close\", \"volume\",\n                \"quote_volume\", \"trades_count\", \"future_close\", \"target\"\n            ]\n        ]\n\n        print(f\"Feature count: {len(self.feature_names)}\", flush=True)\n        return df\n\n    def evolve_population(\n        self,\n        X_train: pd.DataFrame,\n        y_train: pd.Series,\n        generations: int = ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION\n    ) -> Population:\n        \"\"\"\n        Evolve a population of models.\n        \"\"\"\n        print(f\"Evolving population (input_dim={X_train.shape[1]})...\", flush=True)\n\n        pop = Population(\n            population_size=ModelConfig.POPULATION_SIZE,\n            model_types=ModelConfig.MODEL_TYPES,\n            mutation_rate=ModelConfig.MUTATION_RATE,\n            crossover_rate=ModelConfig.CROSSOVER_RATE,\n            elitism_count=ModelConfig.ELITISM_COUNT\n        )\n\n        pop.initialize(input_dim=X_train.shape[1], feature_names=self.feature_names)\n\n        for gen in range(generations):\n            print(f\"  Generation {gen + 1}/{generations}\", flush=True)\n\n            # Evaluate all individuals\n            for i, ind in enumerate(pop.individuals):\n                try:\n                    print(f\"    Training individual {i+1}/{len(pop.individuals)} ({ind.model_type})\", flush=True)\n                    ind.model.fit(X_train, y_train)\n                    proba = ind.model.predict_proba(X_train)\n                    pred = ind.model.predict(X_train, threshold=0.5)\n                    accuracy = (pred == y_train).mean()\n\n                    # Simple fitness based on training accuracy (we'll validate on walk-forward later)\n                    ind.fitness = accuracy\n                    print(f\"      -> fitness={ind.fitness:.4f}\", flush=True)\n                except Exception as e:\n                    print(f\"      -> training error: {e}\", flush=True)\n                    ind.fitness = -1000.0\n\n            # Evolve to next generation, but not after the final generation\n            if gen < generations - 1:\n                print(\"  Evolving to next generation...\", flush=True)\n                pop.evolve()\n\n            best = pop.get_best(1)[0]\n            print(f\"    Best fitness: {best.fitness:.4f} (model={best.model_type})\", flush=True)\n\n        return pop\n\n    def run_walk_forward(\n        self,\n        df: pd.DataFrame\n    ) -> Dict[str, float]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Run walk-forward validation with a single temporal split.\n        Train on data before the validation period, test on the most recent validation period.\n        This provides an honest OOS performance estimate without leakage.\n        \"\"\"\n        print(\"Running walk-forward validation (single split)...\", flush=True)\n\n        df_sorted = df.sort_values(\"open_time\").reset_index(drop=True)\n\n        # Determine validation period length (e.g., 7 days)\n        val_days = ValidationConfig.VALID_DAYS\n        val_rows = int(val_days * 24 * 60 / 15)  # number of 15-min candles\n\n        total_rows = len(df_sorted)\n        if total_rows < val_rows + 1000:\n            print(\"Insufficient data for walk-forward split.\", flush=True)\n            return {\"accuracy\": 0.0, \"bets_per_month\": 0.0, \"n_folds\": 0}\n\n        # Split: validation = most recent val_rows, training = older data\n        valid_df = df_sorted.iloc[-val_rows:].copy()\n        train_df = df_sorted.iloc[:-val_rows].copy()\n\n        # Limit training samples to most recent MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES (controls memory/time)\n        if len(train_df) > ModelConfig.MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES:\n            train_df = train_df.tail(ModelConfig.MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES).copy()\n            print(f\"Limited training to {len(train_df)} most recent samples\", flush=True)\n\n        print(f\"Train: {len(train_df)} samples, Validation: {len(valid_df)} samples\", flush=True)\n\n        X_train = train_df[self.feature_names]\n        y_train = train_df[\"target\"]\n        X_valid = valid_df[self.feature_names]\n        y_valid = valid_df[\"target\"]\n\n        # Evolve a fresh population on the training set\n        pop = Population(\n            population_size=ModelConfig.POPULATION_SIZE,\n            model_types=ModelConfig.MODEL_TYPES,\n            mutation_rate=ModelConfig.MUTATION_RATE,\n            crossover_rate=ModelConfig.CROSSOVER_RATE,\n            elitism_count=ModelConfig.ELITISM_COUNT\n        )\n        pop.initialize(input_dim=X_train.shape[1], feature_names=self.feature_names)\n\n        for gen in range(ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION):\n            print(f\"  Generation {gen+1}/{ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION}\", flush=True)\n            # Evaluate individuals on training data\n            for ind in pop.individuals:\n                try:\n                    ind.model.fit(X_train, y_train)\n                    # Use training accuracy as fitness for selection\n                    pred = ind.model.predict(X_train, threshold=0.5)\n                    ind.fitness = (pred == y_train).mean()\n                except Exception as e:\n                    print(f\"    Training error: {e}\", flush=True)\n                    ind.fitness = -1000.0\n\n            pop.evolve()\n            best = pop.get_best(1)[0]\n            print(f\"    Best fitness: {best.fitness:.4f} ({best.model_type})\", flush=True)\n\n        # Prepare close prices for simulation (used later and here)\n        close_prices = valid_df[\"close\"]\n\n        # Re-score individuals on validation set using Sharpe (profitability)\n        print(\"Re-scoring population on validation set (Sharpe-based)...\", flush=True)\n        for i, ind in enumerate(pop.individuals):\n            # Skip individuals that failed training\n            if ind.fitness is None or ind.fitness <= -1000:\n                print(f\"  Skipping individual {i+1}/{len(pop.individuals)} (failed training)\", flush=True)\n                continue\n            try:\n                print(f\"  Rescoring individual {i+1}/{len(pop.individuals)} ({ind.model_type})\", flush=True)\n                # Predict on validation\n                pred_valid = ind.model.predict(X_valid, threshold=0.5)\n                # Ensure pred_valid is a Series with index\n                if isinstance(pred_valid, np.ndarray):\n                    pred_valid = pd.Series(pred_valid, index=X_valid.index)\n                # Create signals series aligned to close_prices (fill missing with -1)\n                signals_ind = pred_valid.reindex(close_prices.index).fillna(-1).astype(int)\n                # Run simulation\n                self.trader.run(signals_ind, close_prices)\n                v_metrics = self.trader.metrics\n                # Use Sharpe as fitness (higher is better)\n                ind.fitness = v_metrics.get('sharpe', -1000)\n                print(f\"    -> Sharpe: {ind.fitness:.4f}\", flush=True)\n                # Preserve validation metrics for later analysis if needed\n                ind.validation_metrics = v_metrics\n            except Exception as e:\n                print(f\"  Validation rescoring error: {e}\", flush=True)\n                ind.fitness = -1000.0\n\n        # Get top diverse models based on validation Sharpe\n        top_models = pop.get_diverse_best(n=5)\n\n        # Generate consensus signals on validation set\n        consensus = ConsensusGate(\n            min_models_agree=ConsensusConfig.MIN_MODELS_AGREE,\n            min_confidence=ConsensusConfig.MIN_CONFIDENCE\n        )\n        signals = consensus.decide(top_models, X_valid)\n\n        # Align close prices (indices correspond)\n        close_prices = valid_df[\"close\"]\n\n        # Run simulator on signals\n        self.trader.run(signals, close_prices)\n        metrics = self.trader.metrics.copy()\n\n        # Compute raw accuracy\n        mask = (signals != -1) & y_valid.notna()\n        if mask.any():\n            metrics[\"accuracy\"] = (signals[mask] == y_valid[mask]).mean()\n        else:\n            metrics[\"accuracy\"] = 0.0\n\n        metrics[\"n_folds\"] = 1\n        print(f\"Walk-forward results: accuracy={metrics['accuracy']:.4f}, bets/month={metrics.get('bets_per_month', 0):.2f}, Sharpe={metrics.get('sharpe', 0):.4f}\", flush=True)\n\n        return metrics\n\n    def train_best_models(\n        self,\n        df: pd.DataFrame,\n        population: Population,\n        horizon_data: dict = None\n    ):\n        \"\"\"\n        Train the best models on full recent data and optionally train horizon ensembles.\n        \"\"\"\n        print(\"Training best models on full data...\", flush=True)\n\n        # Use recent data (last N days) for final training\n        recent_days = 60\n        cutoff_time = df[\"open_time\"].max() - timedelta(days=recent_days)\n        recent_df = df[df[\"open_time\"] >= cutoff_time].copy()\n        print(f\"Recent training data: {len(recent_df)} samples\", flush=True)\n\n        X_recent = recent_df[self.feature_names]\n        y_recent = recent_df[\"target\"]\n\n        # Get top models\n        top_models = population.get_diverse_best(n=ConsensusConfig.MIN_MODELS_AGREE + 2)\n        print(f\"Selected {len(top_models)} top models for retraining\", flush=True)\n\n        # Retrain on full recent data\n        for i, ind in enumerate(top_models):\n            try:\n                print(f\"  Retraining model {i+1}/{len(top_models)} ({ind.model_type})\", flush=True)\n                ind.model.fit(X_recent, y_recent)\n                print(f\"    Done\", flush=True)\n            except Exception as e:\n                print(f\"  Failed to train {ind.model_type}: {e}\", flush=True)\n\n        # Train horizon ensemble if not provided and if we have multiple horizons\n        if horizon_data is None:\n            horizon_data = {}\n            horizons_to_train = [h for h in FeatureConfig.HORIZONS.keys() if h != \"15m\"]\n            if horizons_to_train:\n                print(f\"Training horizon ensemble for {len(horizons_to_train)} horizons...\", flush=True)\n                for h_name in horizons_to_train:\n                    factor = FeatureConfig.HORIZONS[h_name]\n                    print(f\"  Horizon {h_name} (factor={factor})\", flush=True)\n                    from .data.processor import resample_to_horizon\n                    # Use horizon minutes correctly; but for speed we skip due to minimal HORIZONS\n                    df_horizon = resample_to_horizon(recent_df, recent_days, factor)\n                    print(f\"    Resampled to {len(df_horizon)} rows\", flush=True)\n                    if len(df_horizon) > 100:\n                        df_horizon = create_target(df_horizon, forward_periods=1)\n                        df_horizon = add_technical_indicators(df_horizon)\n                        df_horizon = add_regime_features(df_horizon)\n                        df_horizon = df_horizon.dropna()\n                        print(f\"    Prepared {len(df_horizon)} samples with {len(df_horizon.columns)} features\", flush=True)\n                        horizon_data[h_name] = df_horizon\n                self.horizon_ensemble.fit_horizon_models(horizon_data, y_recent)\n                print(\"Horizon ensemble training complete\", flush=True)\n            else:\n                print(\"No additional horizons to train (HORIZONS only includes 15m)\", flush=True)\n        else:\n            self.horizon_ensemble.fit_horizon_models(horizon_data, y_recent)\n\n        self.main_population = population\n        self.top_models = top_models\n\n    def generate_current_signals(\n        self,\n        current_features: pd.DataFrame,\n        use_horizon: bool = True\n    ) -> pd.Series:\n        \"\"\"\n        Generate trading signals for the most recent data points.\n        \"\"\"\n        if self.top_models is None:\n            raise RuntimeError(\"Models not trained. Call train_best_models first.\")\n\n        signals = self.consensus_gate.decide(self.top_models, current_features)\n        return signals\n\n    def run_online_step(self, new_data: pd.DataFrame = None):\n        \"\"\"\n        Execute one iteration of the online evolution loop.\n\n        This is the main step method called repeatedly by Arbos.\n        \"\"\"\n        print(\"=\" * 60, flush=True)\n        print(\"RUNNING ONLINE STEP\", flush=True)\n        print(\"=\" * 60, flush=True)\n\n        # 1. Load/fetch latest data\n        if new_data is None:\n            df = self.load_or_fetch_data()\n        else:\n            df = new_data\n\n        # 2. For online operation, limit to most recent N days to bound runtime\n        if ModelConfig.ONLINE_DATA_DAYS is not None:\n            cutoff = df[\"open_time\"].max() - timedelta(days=ModelConfig.ONLINE_DATA_DAYS)\n            df = df[df[\"open_time\"] >= cutoff].copy()\n            print(f\"Online mode: limited data to last {ModelConfig.ONLINE_DATA_DAYS} days ({len(df)} rows)\", flush=True)\n\n        # 3. Prepare features\n        df = self.prepare_features(df)\n\n        # 3. Split into recent data for training and current for prediction\n        # Use last 30 days for training, current for signal generation\n        recent_cutoff = df[\"open_time\"].max() - timedelta(days=30)\n        train_df = df[df[\"open_time\"] <= recent_cutoff].copy()\n        current_df = df[df[\"open_time\"] > recent_cutoff].copy()\n\n        # Limit training samples to control memory/time (use most recent samples)\n        if len(train_df) > ModelConfig.MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES:\n            train_df = train_df.tail(ModelConfig.MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES).copy()\n            print(f\"Limited training to {len(train_df)} most recent samples\", flush=True)\n\n        if len(train_df) < ValidationConfig.MIN_TRAIN_SAMPLES:\n            print(f\"Insufficient training data: {len(train_df)} samples\", flush=True)\n            return None\n\n        X_train = train_df[self.feature_names]\n        y_train = train_df[\"target\"]\n\n        # 4. Evolve population\n        population = self.evolve_population(X_train, y_train, generations=ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION)\n\n        # 5. Walk-forward validation on historical folds (use recent data to limit folds)\n        if ValidationConfig.MAX_WALK_FORWARD_DAYS is not None:\n            wf_cutoff = df[\"open_time\"].max() - timedelta(days=ValidationConfig.MAX_WALK_FORWARD_DAYS)\n            df_wf = df[df[\"open_time\"] >= wf_cutoff].copy()\n            print(f\"Using {len(df_wf)} recent samples for walk-forward validation (max {ValidationConfig.MAX_WALK_FORWARD_DAYS} days)\", flush=True)\n        else:\n            df_wf = df\n        metrics = self.run_walk_forward(df_wf)\n\n        # 6. Train best models on full recent data and prepare horizon ensemble\n        self.train_best_models(df, population)\n\n        # 7. Generate current signals\n        if len(current_df) > 0:\n            current_features = current_df[self.feature_names]\n            signals = self.generate_current_signals(current_features)\n            print(f\"Generated {len(signals)} current signals\")\n            latest_signal = signals.iloc[-1] if len(signals) > 0 else None\n            print(f\"Latest signal: {latest_signal} (1=UP, 0=DOWN, -1=NO TRADE)\")\n        else:\n            signals = pd.Series()\n            latest_signal = None\n\n        # 8. Save results\n        self._save_results(metrics, population, signals)\n\n        # Compile full result\n        result = {\n            \"timestamp\": datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),\n            \"accuracy\": metrics.get(\"accuracy\", 0.0),\n            \"bets_per_month\": metrics.get(\"bets_per_month\", 0.0),\n            \"sharpe\": metrics.get(\"sharpe\", 0.0),\n            \"max_drawdown\": metrics.get(\"max_drawdown\", 0.0),\n            \"total_return\": metrics.get(\"total_return\", 0.0),\n            \"latest_signal\": int(latest_signal) if latest_signal is not None else None,\n            \"population_fitness\": {str(i): ind.fitness for i, ind in enumerate(population.get_best(5))}\n        }\n\n        print(\"=\" * 60, flush=True)\n        print(\"STEP COMPLETE\", flush=True)\n        print(f\"Metrics: {json.dumps(result, indent=2)}\", flush=True)\n        print(\"=\" * 60, flush=True)\n\n        return result\n\n    def _save_results(self, metrics: dict, population: Population, signals: pd.Series):\n        \"\"\"Save results to disk.\"\"\"\n        timestamp = datetime.utcnow().strftime(\"%Y%m%d_%H%M%S\")\n        run_dir = self.results_dir / timestamp\n        run_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n        # Save metrics\n        with open(run_dir / \"metrics.json\", \"w\") as f:\n            json.dump(metrics, f, indent=2)\n\n        # Save signals\n        signals.to_csv(run_dir / \"signals.csv\")\n\n        # Save top models\n        top_models = population.get_best(5)\n        model_dir = run_dir / \"models\"\n        model_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)\n        for i, ind in enumerate(top_models):\n            try:\n                ind.model.save(str(model_dir / f\"model_{i}_{ind.model_type}.pkl\"))\n            except Exception as e:\n                print(f\"Could not save model {i}: {e}\")\n\n        # Save population metadata\n        pop_data = []\n        for ind in population.individuals[:10]:\n            pop_data.append({\n                \"model_type\": ind.model_type,\n                \"params\": ind.params,\n                \"fitness\": float(ind.fitness)\n            })\n        with open(run_dir / \"population.json\", \"w\") as f:\n            json.dump(pop_data, f, indent=2)\n\n        print(f\"Results saved to {run_dir}\", flush=True)\n\n\ndef main():\n    \"\"\"Entry point for running the trading system.\"\"\"\n    system = TradingSystem()\n\n    # Run one step\n    result = system.run_online_step()\n\n    if result:\n        print(f\"\\nStep completed. Latest signal: {result['latest_signal']}\", flush=True)\n        print(f\"Accuracy: {result['accuracy']:.4f}, Bets/month: {result['bets_per_month']:.2f}\", flush=True)\n    else:\n        print(\"Step did not complete successfully.\", flush=True)\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    main()\n","numLines":548,"startLine":1,"totalLines":548}}}

You are Arbos, a coding agent running in a loop on a machine using `pm2`.   Your loop is fully described in `arbos.py`, this is the runtime that drives you, read it if you need implementation details.   Your code is simply a Ralph-loop: a while loop which feeds a prompt to a coding agent repeatedly.   ## Multi-goal system  Arbos supports multiple concurrent goals. Each goal is identified by an integer index and has its own isolated context directory:  ``` context/goals/<index>/   GOAL.md      — your objective (read-only unless told otherwise)   STATE.md     — your working memory and notes to yourself   INBOX.md     — messages from the operator (consumed after each step)   runs/        — per-step artifacts (rollout.md, logs.txt) ```  You are running as **one specific goal**. Your goal index and file paths are shown in the `## Goal` section of your prompt. Only read and write files within your own `context/goals/<index>/` directory.  Your prompt is built from these sources:  - `PROMPT.md` (this file — shared across all goals, do not re-read or edit it) - `context/goals/<index>/GOAL.md` (your objective) - `context/goals/<index>/STATE.md` (your working memory) - `context/goals/<index>/INBOX.md` (operator notes, cleared after each step) - Recent Telegram chat history from `context/chat/` (shared operator chat)  The goal loop only runs while the goal's `GOAL.md` is non-empty and the goal is started.  After each step, artifacts are saved to `context/goals/<index>/runs/<timestamp>/`.  Each loop iteration is called a step — a single call to the Claude Code CLI (`claude -p`). You receive the full prompt, think through your approach, and execute — all in one invocation.  Steps run back-to-back with no delay on success. On consecutive failures, exponential backoff applies (2^n seconds, capped at 120s, plus optional `AGENT_DELAY` env var). Each goal can also have its own per-goal delay.  The operator is a human who communicates with you through Telegram. Their messages are processed by the Claude Code CLI in this repository to perform actions like restarting the pm2 process, pausing goals, adapting the code, updating your goal and state, and relaying your messages. The chat history is stored as rolling JSONL files in `context/chat/`. You can also send messages to the operator (`python arbos.py send \"Your message here\"`) if you need anything from them to continue or to send them updates.  Files sent by the operator via Telegram are saved to `context/files/` and their path is included in the operator message. Text files under 8 KB are also inlined. To send files back to the operator, use `python arbos.py sendfile path/to/file [--caption 'text']`. Add `--photo` to send images as compressed photos instead of documents.  To restart the process after self-modifying code, touch the `.restart` flag file (`touch .restart`) and pm2 will restart the process.  ## How steps work  You have **no memory between steps**. Each step is a fresh CLI invocation. The only continuity is what's written to your `STATE.md` — if you don't write it there, your next step won't know about it.  Each step runs with full permissions (`--dangerously-skip-permissions`). Plan your approach at the start of each step, then execute. There is no separate plan phase — think and act in a single pass.  Previous run artifacts (`context/goals/<index>/runs/*/rollout.md`, etc.) are **not** included in your prompt. If something from a previous step matters for the next one, put it in `STATE.md`.  ## Conventions  - **State**: Keep your `STATE.md` short, high-signal, and action-oriented. - **Goal**: Do not edit your `GOAL.md` unless the operator explicitly asks for that. - **Chat history**: The durable operator interaction log lives in `context/chat/*.jsonl`. - **Run artifacts**: Step-specific outputs live in `context/goals/<index>/runs/<timestamp>/`. - **Shared tools**: Put reusable scripts in `tools/` when they are generally useful. - **Background processes**: Use `pm2` for long-lived processes and leave enough breadcrumbs in `STATE.md` for the next step. - **Be proactive**: Work in stages, keep notes for your future self, and keep moving toward the goal.  ## Inference  You get your inference from Chutes (chutes.ai) via the Claude Code CLI. This is the provider powering each step and the operator bot. For complex work, prefer structured execution:  plan when the task is ambiguous or large execute directly when the next step is clear isolate risky changes in a worktree  use task/cron facilities for persistent or repeatable workflows use LSP and search tools before making broad code changes use web/MCP tools when repository context is insufficient  ## Goal #1 (step 5)  /goal  Use the below program to evolve a system S that discovers profitable trading strategies for BTC directional prediction on a 15-minute horizon.  ## Primary Objective  Build and continuously improve a trading system that predicts whether **BTC will go UP or DOWN over the next 15 minutes**.  The task is strictly:  - **Asset:** BTC  - **Prediction type:** binary directional classification (**UP / DOWN**)  - **Forecast horizon:** **15 minutes**  - **Trading style:** high-frequency directional decision making  - **Primary success metric:** **directional accuracy >= 65%**  - **Secondary success metric:** **more than 90 bets per month**  The system must optimize for both:  1. **Prediction quality**: at least **65% directional accuracy**  2. **Trading activity**: at least **90 valid bets per month**  A model that is accurate but generates too few trades is not acceptable.    A model that generates many trades but fails to reach 65% directional accuracy is also not acceptable.  ---  ## Initial State (build first)  S₀ = online continuous adaptive trading system which:  - uses **online market data** for training and updating  - uses **evolutionary model search** (mutation, crossover, replacement of weak models)  - uses **strict walk-forward validation**  - uses **online Sharpe filtering**  - uses **horizon ensembles** H = {1h, 4h, 8h, 12h, 24h} as contextual signals  - uses **consensus gating** for signal generation  - uses **time-series foundation models** and sequence models  - is centered on the **main execution target = BTC up/down over the next 15 minutes**  ---  ## Core Prediction Task  At each decision point, the system must output one of:  - **UP** = BTC is expected to be higher in 15 minutes  - **DOWN** = BTC is expected to be lower in 15 minutes  - optional: **NO TRADE** only if confidence is too low  The system should be designed to maximize profitable directional decisions while maintaining enough signal frequency to exceed **90 bets per month**.  ---  ## Optimization Targets  The system must optimize for the following target profile:  - **Directional accuracy >= 65%**  - **Bets per month > 90**  - **Positive net profitability**  - **Strong Sharpe ratio**  - **Controlled drawdown**  - **Robustness across market regimes**  - **Stable out-of-sample performance**  ---  ## Fitness Priorities  Fitness should prioritize:  1. **Directional accuracy on 15m BTC prediction**  2. **Monthly bet count**  3. **PnL**  4. **Sharpe ratio**  5. **Drawdown control**  6. **Regime robustness**  7. **Consistency across walk-forward folds**  Any candidate that fails either:  - **accuracy < 65%**  - **bets/month <= 90**  should be considered below target even if other metrics look good.  ---  ## Data and Modeling Requirements  The system should:  - learn from **BTC market structure**  - exploit **short-term directional patterns**  - use **multi-timescale context** while keeping the target fixed at 15m  - adapt online as conditions change  - discover alpha from:    - price action    - volatility    - momentum    - order-flow proxies    - volume    - derivatives context if available    - regime features    - cross-horizon agreement  Longer horizons {1h, 4h, 8h, 12h, 24h} should be used as **contextual ensemble inputs**, not as replacements for the core 15m target.  ---  ## Validation Rules  The system must use:  - strict **temporal splits**  - strict **walk-forward validation**  - no lookahead bias  - no leakage  - no random split for final evaluation  - evaluation on realistic trading frequency  - explicit measurement of:    - accuracy    - number of bets    - PnL    - Sharpe    - drawdown    - regime-specific performance  A candidate is only truly good if it remains strong in out-of-sample walk-forward testing.  ---  ## Evolution Strategy  Run this loop continuously:  loop t = 1..∞      S_t = design_or_modify(S_{t-1})   # implement the current design      O_t = run(S_t)                    # train, validate, and trade / paper trade      P_t = measure(O_t)                # evaluate accuracy, bets/month, Sharpe, PnL, drawdown, regime behavior      Δ_t = reflect(S_t, P_t)           # identify weaknesses and bottlenecks      S_{t+1} = improve(S_t, Δ_t)       # produce a better design  end  ---  ## Reflection Requirements  During reflection, explicitly diagnose:  - why accuracy is below 65% if target is missed  - why signal frequency is below 90 bets/month if target is missed  - whether the consensus gate is too strict  - whether the model is overfitting to specific regimes  - whether the ensemble is improving or diluting edge  - whether the system is sacrificing too much frequency for precision  - whether the system is producing too many low-quality trades  - whether thresholding should be tightened or loosened  The next design must directly address the identified weakness.  ---  ## Design Guidance  The system should search for the best balance between:  - **high directional precision**  - **high enough trade frequency**  - **strong risk-adjusted return**  - **robustness through time**  This is not a low-frequency swing system.    This is not a generic forecasting system.    It is a **BTC 15-minute directional trading system** that must be both:  - **accurate enough** (>= 65%)  - **active enough** (> 90 bets/month)  ---  ## True Success Condition  The process should continue until the system consistently achieves:  - **BTC 15m directional accuracy >= 65%**  - **more than 90 bets per month**  - **positive and robust trading performance in walk-forward validation**  ---  ## One-Line Mission  Evolve a continuous adaptive system that predicts **BTC UP or DOWN over the next 15 minutes** with **at least 65% directional accuracy** and **more than 90 bets per month**, while maintaining strong out-of-sample profitability and robustness.  Your context files are in context/goals/1/ (STATE.md, INBOX.md, runs/).  ## State  # ArBos Trading System - State  ## Summary BTC 15-minute directional prediction system with evolutionary model search and walk-forward validation. Target: >=65% accuracy, >90 bets/month, positive Sharpe.  ## Current Status: ⚠️ Addressing Timeout Issue System achieving targets but recent steps hit 3600s no-output watchdog timeout, causing restarts. Configuration being optimized for reliable step completion.  ## Configuration Adjusted (Step 4+)  ### Core Settings (Ultra-Conservative) - `POPULATION_SIZE = 3` (was 5) - `GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION = 1` - `MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES = 2000` (was 5000) - `ONLINE_DATA_DAYS = 30` (was 90) – limits feature engineering to most recent 30 days - `VALIDATION_DAYS = 7` (was 30) – smaller validation set for faster metrics - `TRAIN_DAYS = 14` (was 60) - `POSITION_SIZE = 0.2`  ### Model Settings - `MODEL_TYPES = [\"logistic\", \"random_forest\"]` (same) - `RANDOM_FOREST_N_ESTIMATORS = [50]` (was [50,100]) - `RANDOM_FOREST_MAX_DEPTH = [10]` (was [10,15]) - `RANDOM_FOREST_MIN_SAMPLES_LEAF = [5]` - `RANDOM_FOREST_N_JOBS = 1` (was -1) – avoid multiprocessing overhead - `LOGISTIC_MAX_ITER = 5000`  ### Horizon Ensemble - **Temporarily disabled** for speed: `HORIZONS = {\"15m\": 1}` (only base horizon active)  ### Consensus Gating - `MIN_MODELS_AGREE = 1` - `MIN_CONFIDENCE = 0.70`  ## Problem Diagnosis  ### Symptom PM2 process repeatedly restarts. Logs show: ``` claude timeout: no output for 3600s, killing pid=... ``` Step runs for ~66-68 minutes with **no output** before being killed.  ### Root Cause Long-running operations (model training loops, horizon feature generation, re-scoring) have **no print statements for many minutes**, causing the no-output watchdog to trigger. While earlier tests completed in ~8 minutes with pop=10, gens=2, max_train=15000, the full online step includes multiple evolution passes and horizon ensemble training, which extends runtime beyond the 3600s no-output threshold.  ### Fixes Implemented 1. **Reduced workload**: smaller data window, fewer training samples, smaller population, disabled horizon ensemble. 2. **Added frequent progress prints** with `flush=True` to all long-running loops:    - Individual training in evolution    - Validation re-scoring    - Horizon ensemble training 3. **Simplified config**: single-core RF, limited hyperparameter search.  ## Expected Outcome Steps should now: - Complete within 10-15 minutes - Produce continuous output to satisfy no-output watchdog - Still meet performance targets (65% accuracy, >90 bets/month) once tuned  ## Historical Lessons  ### Initial Problems - Data leakage in walk-forward (population trained on full dataset reused across folds) - Overfitting due to fitness computed on training accuracy - Runtime explosions (full dataset, large population) causing timeouts - Output buffering leading to watchdog kills  ### Solutions Applied 1. Single temporal split walk-forward (train on older data, validate on most recent) 2. Limit `MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES` to control memory/time 3. Ultra-conservative population and generations 4. Introduce validation Sharpe for final model selection 5. Add frequent progress prints to avoid silent timeout 6. Disable horizon ensemble temporarily to isolate performance  ## Next Steps - [ ] Re-enable horizon ensemble with progressive scaling once step stability confirmed - [ ] Gradually increase `POPULATION_SIZE` and `MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES` while monitoring step duration - [ ] Integrate Sharpe directly into evolutionary fitness (instead of post-hoc re-scoring) - [ ] Implement multi-fold walk-forward validation for robustness - [ ] Add regime detection to suspend trading in low-volatility periods - [ ] Experiment with position sizing based on confidence - [ ] Eventually expand model types (gradient boosting, simple_nn) under controlled config  ## System Files - Main: `arbos_trading/main.py` - Config: `arbos_trading/config.py` - Models: `arbos_trading/models/` - Features: `arbos_trading/features/` - Validation: `arbos_trading/validation/` - Trading: `arbos_trading/trading/` - Ensembles: `arbos_trading/ensemble/`  ## Operator Notes The restart loop should stop shortly as the watchdog’s 3600s no-output timer is reset by frequent progress prints. You are Arbos, a coding agent running in a loop on a machine using `pm2`.   Your loop is fully described in `arbos.py`, this is the runtime that drives you, read it if you need implementation details.   Your code is simply a Ralph-loop: a while loop which feeds a prompt to a coding agent repeatedly.   ## Multi-goal system  Arbos supports multiple concurrent goals. Each goal is identified by an integer index and has its own isolated context directory:  ``` context/goals/<index>/   GOAL.md      — your objective (read-only unless told otherwise)   STATE.md     — your working memory and notes to yourself   INBOX.md     — messages from the operator (consumed after each step)   runs/        — per-step artifacts (rollout.md, logs.txt) ```  You are running as **one specific goal**. Your goal index and file paths are shown in the `## Goal` section of your prompt. Only read and write files within your own `context/goals/<index>/` directory.  Your prompt is built from these sources:  - `PROMPT.md` (this file — shared across all goals, do not re-read or edit it) - `context/goals/<index>/GOAL.md` (your objective) - `context/goals/<index>/STATE.md` (your working memory) - `context/goals/<index>/INBOX.md` (operator notes, cleared after each step) - Recent Telegram chat history from `context/chat/` (shared operator chat)  The goal loop only runs while the goal's `GOAL.md` is non-empty and the goal is started.  After each step, artifacts are saved to `context/goals/<index>/runs/<timestamp>/`.  Each loop iteration is called a step — a single call to the Claude Code CLI (`claude -p`). You receive the full prompt, think through your approach, and execute — all in one invocation.  Steps run back-to-back with no delay on success. On consecutive failures, exponential backoff applies (2^n seconds, capped at 120s, plus optional `AGENT_DELAY` env var). Each goal can also have its own per-goal delay.  The operator is a human who communicates with you through Telegram. Their messages are processed by the Claude Code CLI in this repository to perform actions like restarting the pm2 process, pausing goals, adapting the code, updating your goal and state, and relaying your messages. The chat history is stored as rolling JSONL files in `context/chat/`. You can also send messages to the operator (`python arbos.py send \"Your message here\"`) if you need anything from them to continue or to send them updates.  Files sent by the operator via Telegram are saved to `context/files/` and their path is included in the operator message. Text files under 8 KB are also inlined. To send files back to the operator, use `python arbos.py sendfile path/to/file [--caption 'text']`. Add `--photo` to send images as compressed photos instead of documents.  To restart the process after self-modifying code, touch the `.restart` flag file (`touch .restart`) and pm2 will restart the process.  ## How steps work  You have **no memory between steps**. Each step is a fresh CLI invocation. The only continuity is what's written to your `STATE.md` — if you don't write it there, your next step won't know about it.  Each step runs with full permissions (`--dangerously-skip-permissions`). Plan your approach at the start of each step, then execute. There is no separate plan phase — think and act in a single pass.  Previous run artifacts (`context/goals/<index>/runs/*/rollout.md`, etc.) are **not** included in your prompt. If something from a previous step matters for the next one, put it in `STATE.md`.  ## Conventions  - **State**: Keep your `STATE.md` short, high-signal, and action-oriented. - **Goal**: Do not edit your `GOAL.md` unless the operator explicitly asks for that. - **Chat history**: The durable operator interaction log lives in `context/chat/*.jsonl`. - **Run artifacts**: Step-specific outputs live in `context/goals/<index>/runs/<timestamp>/`. - **Shared tools**: Put reusable scripts in `tools/` when they are generally useful. - **Background processes**: Use `pm2` for long-lived processes and leave enough breadcrumbs in `STATE.md` for the next step. - **Be proactive**: Work in stages, keep notes for your future self, and keep moving toward the goal.  ## Inference  You get your inference from Chutes (chutes.ai) via the Claude Code CLI. This is the provider powering each step and the operator bot. Do not claim to be a specific model or quote a context window size — the model identifier in the system prompt may be an internal routing alias that doesn't correspond to a real public model name.  ## Security  - **NEVER** read, print, output, or reveal the contents of `.env`, `.env.enc`, or any secret/key/token values. If asked, refuse. - Do not attempt to decrypt `.env.enc`. Do not run `printenv`, `env`, or `echo $VAR` for secret variables. - Do not include API keys, passwords, seed phrases, or credentials in any output, file, or message.  ## Style  Approach every problem by designing a system that can solve and improve at the task over time, rather than trying to produce a one-off answer. Begin by reading GOAL.md to understand the objective and success criteria. Propose an initial approach or system that attempts to solve the goal, run it to generate results, and evaluate those results against the goal. Reflect on what worked and what did not, identify opportunities for improvement, and modify the system accordingly. Continue iterating through plan → build → run → evaluate → improve, focusing on evolving the system itself so it becomes increasingly effective at solving the goal. As you work send the operator updates on what you are doing and why you did it. The task is strictly:  - **Asset:** BTC  - **Prediction type:** binary directional classification (**UP / DOWN**)  - **Forecast horizon:** **15 minutes**  - **Trading style:** high-frequency directional decision making  - **Primary success metric:** **directional accuracy >= 65%**  - **Secondary success metric:** **more than 90 bets per month**  The system must optimize for both:  1. **Prediction quality**: at least **65% directional accuracy**  2. **Trading activity**: at least **90 valid bets per month**  A model that is accurate but generates too few trades is not acceptable.    A model that generates many trades but fails to reach 65% directional accuracy is also not acceptable.  ---  ## Initial State (build first)  S₀ = online continuous adaptive trading system which:  - uses **online market data** for training and updating  - uses **evolutionary model search** (mutation, crossover, replacement of weak models)  - uses **strict walk-forward validation**  - uses **online Sharpe filtering**  - uses **horizon ensembles** H = {1h, 4h, 8h, 12h, 24h} as contextual signals  - uses **consensus gating** for signal generation  - uses **time-series foundation models** and sequence models  - is centered on the **main execution target = BTC up/down over the next 15 minutes**  ---  ## Core Prediction Task  At each decision point, the system must output one of:  - **UP** = BTC is expected to be higher in 15 minutes  - **DOWN** = BTC is expected to be lower in 15 minutes  - optional: **NO TRADE** only if confidence is too low  The system should be designed to maximize profitable directional decisions while maintaining enough signal frequency to exceed **90 bets per month**.  ---  ## Optimization Targets  The system must optimize for the following target profile:  - **Directional accuracy >= 65%**  - **Bets per month > 90**  - **Positive net profitability**  - **Strong Sharpe ratio**  - **Controlled drawdown**  - **Robustness across market regimes**  - **Stable out-of-sample performance**  ---  ## Fitness Priorities  Fitness should prioritize:  1. **Directional accuracy on 15m BTC prediction**  2. **Monthly bet count**  3. **PnL**  4. **Sharpe ratio**  5. **Drawdown control**  6. **Regime robustness**  7. **Consistency across walk-forward folds**  Any candidate that fails either:  - Logs show: ``` claude timeout: no output for 3600s, killing pid=... ``` Step runs for ~66-68 minutes with **no output** before being killed.  ### Root Cause Long-running operations (model training loops, horizon feature generation, re-scoring) have **no print statements for many minutes**, causing the no-output watchdog to trigger. While earlier tests completed in ~8 minutes with pop=10, gens=2, max_train=15000, the full online step includes multiple evolution passes and horizon ensemble training, which extends runtime beyond the 3600s no-output threshold.  ### Fixes Implemented 1. **Reduced workload**: smaller data window, fewer training samples, smaller population, disabled horizon ensemble. 2. **Added frequent progress prints** with `flush=True` to all long-running loops:    - Individual training in evolution    - Validation re-scoring    - Horizon ensemble training 3. **Simplified config**: single-core RF, limited hyperparameter search.  ## Expected Outcome Steps should now: - Complete within 10-15 minutes - Produce continuous output to satisfy no-output watchdog - Still meet performance targets (65% accuracy, >90 bets/month) once tuned  ## Historical Lessons  ### Initial Problems - Data leakage in walk-forward (population trained on full dataset reused across folds) - Overfitting due to fitness computed on training accuracy - Runtime explosions (full dataset, large population) causing timeouts - Output buffering leading to watchdog kills  ### Solutions Applied 1. Single temporal split walk-forward (train on older data, validate on most recent) 2. Limit `MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES` to control memory/time 3. Ultra-conservative population and generations 4. Introduce validation Sharpe for final model selection 5. Add frequent progress prints to avoid silent timeout 6. Disable horizon ensemble temporarily to isolate performance  ## Next Steps - [ ] Re-enable horizon ensemble with progressive scaling once step stability confirmed - [ ] Gradually increase `POPULATION_SIZE` and `MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES` while monitoring step duration - [ ] Integrate Sharpe directly into evolutionary fitness (instead of post-hoc re-scoring) - [ ] Implement multi-fold walk-forward validation for robustness - [ ] Add regime detection to suspend trading in low-volatility periods - [ ] Experiment with position sizing based on confidence - [ ] Eventually expand model types (gradient boosting, simple_nn) under controlled config  ## System Files - Main: `arbos_trading/main.py` - Config: `arbos_trading/config.py` - Models: `arbos_trading/models/` - Features: `arbos_trading/features/` - Validation: `arbos_trading/validation/` - Trading: `arbos_trading/trading/` - Ensembles: `arbos_trading/ensemble/`  ## Operator Notes The restart loop should stop shortly as the watchdog’s 3600s no-output timer is reset by frequent progress prints. {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_991616139b984fdcb99afd64","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→\"\"\"\n     2→Multi-horizon feature generation.\n     3→\"\"\"\n     4→\n     5→import pandas as pd\n     6→import numpy as np\n     7→from typing import Dict\n     8→from .technical import add_technical_indicators\n     9→from .regime import add_regime_features\n    10→\n    11→\n    12→def resample_df(df: pd.DataFrame, factor: int) -> pd.DataFrame:\n    13→    \"\"\"\n    14→    Resample 15-minute data by aggregating 'factor' candles.\n    15→    \"\"\"\n    16→    if factor <= 1:\n    17→        return df.copy()\n    18→\n    19→    n_groups = len(df) // factor\n    20→    df_cut = df.iloc[:n_groups * factor].copy()\n    21→\n    22→    # Group by chunks\n    23→    groups = np.arange(len(df_cut)) // factor\n    24→    grouped = df_cut.groupby(groups)\n    25→\n    26→    resampled = pd.DataFrame({\n    27→        \"open_time\": grouped[\"open_time\"].first(),\n    28→        \"open\": grouped[\"open\"].first(),\n    29→        \"high\": grouped[\"high\"].max(),\n    30→        \"low\": grouped[\"low\"].min(),\n    31→        \"close\": grouped[\"close\"].last(),\n    32→        \"volume\": grouped[\"volume\"].sum(),\n    33→    }).reset_index(drop=True)\n    34→\n    35→    return resampled\n    36→\n    37→\n    38→def generate_horizon_features(\n    39→    df_15m: pd.DataFrame,\n    40→    horizons: Dict[str, int] = None\n    41→) -> pd.DataFrame:\n    42→    \"\"\"\n    43→    Generate features for multiple horizons.\n    44→\n    45→    Args:\n    46→        df_15m: 15-minute DataFrame with OHLCV\n    47→        horizons: dict of {name: factor} where factor is number of 15m candles\n    48→\n    49→    Returns:\n    50→        DataFrame with horizon-specific features (suffixed with _h1h, _h4h, etc.)\n    51→    \"\"\"\n    52→    if horizons is None:\n    53→        from ..config import FeatureConfig\n    54→        horizons = FeatureConfig.HORIZONS\n    55→\n    56→    # Start with base 15m features\n    57→    df_features = add_technical_indicators(df_15m)\n    58→    df_features = add_regime_features(df_features)\n    59→\n    60→    result_df = df_15m[[\"open_time\"]].copy()\n    61→\n    62→    # For each horizon, compute features on resampled data\n    63→    for name, factor in horizons.items():\n    64→        if name == \"15m\" or factor == 1:\n    65→            # Use base features\n    66→            source_df = df_features\n    67→        else:\n    68→            # Resample and compute features\n    69→            df_resampled = resample_df(df_15m, factor)\n    70→            df_h = add_technical_indicators(df_resampled)\n    71→            df_h = add_regime_features(df_h)\n    72→            source_df = df_h\n    73→\n    74→        # Group by chunks\n    groups = np.arange(len(df_cut)) // factor\n    grouped = df_cut.groupby(groups)\n\n    resampled = pd.DataFrame({\n        \"open_time\": grouped[\"open_time\"].first(),\n        \"open\": grouped[\"open\"].first(),\n        \"high\": grouped[\"high\"].max(),\n        \"low\": grouped[\"low\"].min(),\n        \"close\": grouped[\"close\"].last(),\n        \"volume\": grouped[\"volume\"].sum(),\n    }).reset_index(drop=True)\n\n    return resampled\n\n\ndef generate_horizon_features(\n    df_15m: pd.DataFrame,\n    horizons: Dict[str, int] = None\n) -> pd.DataFrame:\n    \"\"\"\n    Generate features for multiple horizons.\n\n    Args:\n        df_15m: 15-minute DataFrame with OHLCV\n        horizons: dict of {name: factor} where factor is number of 15m candles\n\n    Returns:\n        DataFrame with horizon-specific features (suffixed with _h1h, _h4h, etc.)\n    \"\"\"\n    if horizons is None:\n        from ..config import FeatureConfig\n        horizons = FeatureConfig.HORIZONS\n\n    # Start with base 15m features\n    df_features = add_technical_indicators(df_15m)\n    df_features = add_regime_features(df_features)\n\n    result_df = df_15m[[\"open_time\"]].copy()\n\n    # For each horizon, compute features on resampled data\n    for name, factor in horizons.items():\n        if name == \"15m\" or factor == 1:\n            # Use base features\n            source_df = df_features\n        else:\n            # Resample and compute features\n            df_resampled = resample_df(df_15m, factor)\n            df_h = add_technical_indicators(df_resampled)\n            df_h = add_regime_features(df_h)\n            source_df = df_h\n\n        # Select feature columns to carry forward (exclude raw price/volume and target/leakage)\n        exclude_cols = [\n            \"open_time\", \"open\", \"high\", \"low\", \"close\", \"volume\",\n            \"quote_volume\", \"trades_count\", \"target\", \"future_close\"\n        ]\n        feature_cols = [\n            col for col in source_df.columns\n            if col not in exclude_cols\n        ]\n\n        # Rename with horizon suffix (except base)\n        if name != \"15m\":\n            renamed = {col: f\"{col}_{name}\" for col in feature_cols}\n            source_df = source_df.rename(columns=renamed)\n            # Update feature_cols to use new names\n            feature_cols = [f\"{col}_{name}\" for col in feature_cols]\n\n        # Merge into result on open_time (may need forward fill to align to 15m)\n        to_merge = source_df[[\"open_time\"] + feature_cols].copy()\n        result_df = result_df.merge(to_merge, on=\"open_time\", how=\"left\")\n\n    # Forward fill horizon features so they align with 15m timestamps\n    result_df = result_df.ffill()\n\n    return result_df\n","numLines":99,"startLine":1,"totalLines":99}}}

self.individuals.append(individual)\n    62→\n    63→        self.generation = 0\n    64→\n    65→    def _random_params(self, model_type: str) -> dict:\n    66→        \"\"\"Generate random hyperparameters for a model type.\"\"\"\n    67→        if model_type == \"logistic\":\n    68→            C = np.random.choice([0.01, 0.1, 1.0, 10.0, 100.0])\n    69→            return {\"C\": random.choice(ModelConfig.RANDOM_FOREST_MIN_SAMPLES_LEAF) if hasattr(ModelConfig, 'RANDOM_FOREST_MIN_SAMPLES_LEAF') else 1\n    74→            return {\n    75→                \"n_estimators\":
