structure\n    88→            sizes = [int(hidden_sizes)] * layers\n    89→            return {\n    90→                \"hidden_sizes\": sizes,\n    91→                \"dropout\": np.random.uniform(0.1, 0.5),\n    92→                \"learning_rate\": np.random.uniform(0.0001, 0.001),\n    93→                \"epochs\": 30\n    94→            }\n    95→        return {}\n    96→\n    97→    def evaluate_fitness(self, individual: Individual, metrics: Dict[str, float]):\n    98→        \"\"\"\n    99→        Compute composite fitness score.\n   100→\n   101→        Fitness = w1*accuracy + w2*bet_ratio + w3*sharpe - w4*drawdown\n   102→        where bet_ratio = (bets_per_month / target_bets) capped at 1\n   103→        \"\"\"\n   104→        accuracy = metrics.get(\"accuracy\", 0.0)\n   105→        bets_per_month = metrics.get(\"bets_per_month\", 0.0)\n   106→        sharpe = metrics.get(\"sharpe\", 0.0)\n   107→        drawdown = metrics.get(\"max_drawdown\", 0.0)\n   108→\n   109→        # Normalize bet count (encourage reaching 90)\n   110→        target_bets = 90.0\n   111→        bet_ratio = min(bets_per_month / target_bets, 1.0)\n   112→\n   113→        # Dual penalty for missing targets\n   114→        accuracy_penalty = 0.0 if accuracy >= 0.65 else (0.65 - accuracy) * 10\n   115→        bet_penalty = 0.0 if bets_per_month >= 90 else (90 - bets_per_month) / 90.0 * 5\n   116→\n   117→        # Weighted sum (tunable)\n   118→        fitness = (\n   119→            2.0 * accuracy +\n   120→            1.0 * bet_ratio +\n   121→            0.5 * max(sharpe, 0) -\n   122→            0.5 * drawdown\n   123→        ) - accuracy_penalty - bet_penalty\n   124→\n   125→        individual.fitness = fitness\n   126→        individual.metrics = metrics.copy()\n   127→\n   128→    def _select_parents(self) -> Tuple[Individual, Individual]:\n   129→        \"\"\"Tournament selection.\"\"\"\n   130→        tournament_size = 3\n   131→\n   132→        def tournament():\n   133→            contestants = random.sample(self.individuals, tournament_size)\n   134→            return max(contestants, key=lambda ind: ind.fitness)\n   135→\n   136→        return tournament(), tournament()\n   137→\n   138→    def _mutate(self, individual: Individual) -> Individual:\n   139→        \"\"\"Apply random mutations to hyperparameters.\"\"\"\n   140→        if random.random() > self.mutation_rate:\n   141→            return individual\n   142→\n   143→        new_params = individual.params.copy()\n   144→        model_type = individual.model_type\n   145→\n   146→        if model_type == \"logistic\":\n   147→            if random.random() < 0.5:\n   148→                new_params[\"C\"] = float(np.random.choice([0.01, 0.1, 1.0, 10.0, 100.0]))\n   149→        elif model_type == \"random_forest\":\n   150→            if random.random() < 0.5:\n   151→                new_params[\"n_estimators\"] = int(np.random.choice(ModelConfig.RANDOM_FOREST_N_ESTIMATORS))\n   152→            if random.random() < 0.5:\n   153→                new_params[\"max_depth\"] = random.choice(ModelConfig.RANDOM_FOREST_MAX_DEPTH)\n   154→            if hasattr(ModelConfig, 'RANDOM_FOREST_MIN_SAMPLES_LEAF'):\n   155→                if random.random() < 0.3:\n   156→                    new_params[\"min_samples_leaf\"] = random.choice(ModelConfig.RANDOM_FOREST_MIN_SAMPLES_LEAF)\n   157→        elif model_type == \"gradient_boosting\":\n   158→            if random.random() < 0.5:\n   159→                new_params[\"learning_rate\"] = float(np.random.choice(ModelConfig.GRADIENT_BOOSTING_LEARNING_RATE))\n   160→            if random.random() < 0.5:\n   161→                new_params[\"n_estimators\"] = int(np.random.choice([50, 100, 200]))\n   162→        elif model_type == \"simple_nn\":\n   163→            if random.random() < 0.3:\n   164→                # Change number of layers\n   165→                layers = random.choice(ModelConfig.SIMPLE_NN_LAYERS)\n   166→                hidden = random.choice(ModelConfig.SIMPLE_NN_HIDDEN_SIZE)\n   167→ \"\"\"\n        Compute composite fitness score.\n\n        Fitness = w1*accuracy + w2*bet_ratio + w3*sharpe - w4*drawdown\n        where bet_ratio = (bets_per_month / target_bets) capped at 1\n )\n\n    def _crossover(self, parent1: Individual, parent2: Individual) -> Individual:\n        \"\"\"Crossover between two parents (blend or swap).\"\"\"\n        if random.random() > Factory function\nMODEL_REGISTRY = {\n    \"logistic\": LogisticModel,\n    \"random_forest\": RandomForestModel,\n    \"gradient_boosting\": GradientBoostingModel,\n    \"simple_nn\": SimpleNNModel,\n}\n\ndef create_model(model_type: str, **kwargs) -> BaseModel:\n    if model_type not in MODEL_REGISTRY:\n        raise ValueError(f\"Unknown model type: {model_type}\")\n    return MODEL_REGISTRY[model_type](**kwargs)\n","numLines":364,"startLine":1,"totalLines":364}}}

But in run_walk_forward, there is no such condition; it calls pop.evolve() unconditionally at the end of each generation loop (line 263). Let's verify: The code snippet we read from main.py lines 250-266 shows:\n\n```\nfor gen in range(ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION):\n    print(f\"  Generation {gen+1}/{ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION}\", flush=True)\n    # Evaluate individuals on training data\n    for ind in pop.individuals:\n        try:\n            ind.model.fit(X_train, y_train)\n            # Use training accuracy as fitness\n            pred = ind.model.predict(X_train, threshold=0.5)\n            ind.fitness = (pred == y_train).mean()\n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f\"    Training error: {e}\", flush=True)\n            ind.fitness = -1000.0\n\n    pop.evolve()\n    best = pop.get_best(1)[0]\n    print(f\"    Best fitness: {best.fitness:.4f} ({best.model_type})\", flush=True)\n```\n\nThat's the code from lines 250-266 as I saw earlier. There is no condition to skip evolve after final generation. So if GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION = 1, then we train all individuals, then call pop.evolve(). That evolve will create a new population (elites + offspring) and replace pop.individuals. The offspring are new individuals with fresh (untrained) models. And we exit the loop after that one iteration. So the final population consists of offspring that have NOT been trained! That's the bug. The logic is: for each generation, we train the current population, evaluate fitness, then evolve to next generation. After the last generation, we should NOT evolve because we want the trained individuals as final result. The evolve method should be called only if we plan to do another generation. So it should be `if gen < generations - 1: pop.evolve()`.\n\nThe evolve_population method (in main.py lines 173-200) has that condition (line 193-195). But run_walk_forward does not. Fetch latest data\\n2. Generate features (including horizon ensembles)\\n3. Evolve model population\\n4. Train best models with walk-forward validation\\n5. Generate signals via consensus gating\\n6. Paper trade and measure performance\\n7. Reflect on metrics and adjust system\\n8. Repeat\\n\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n\\nimport os\\nimport sys\\nimport json\\nimport pickle\\nfrom datetime import datetime, timedelta\\nfrom pathlib import Path\\nfrom typing import Dict, Generator, Tuple, Optional\\nimport pandas as pd\\nimport numpy as np\\n\\nfrom .config import *\\nfrom .data.fetcher import BinanceDataFetcher, fetch_and_save_historical\\nfrom .features.horizon import .models.population import Population, Individual\\nfrom .ensemble.horizon_ensemble import HorizonEnsemble\\nfrom .ensemble.consensus import ConsensusGate\\nfrom .validation.walk_forward import WalkForwardValidator\\nfrom .trading.simulator import SimulatedTrader\\nfrom .trading.metrics import evaluate_predictions\\n\\n\\nclass TradingSystem:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Main trading system class that orchestrates the full workflow.\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n\\n    def __init__(\\n        self,\\n        data_path: str = None,\\n        results_dir: str = PathConfig.RESULTS_DIR,\\n        log_dir: str = PathConfig.LOGS_DIR\\n    ):\\n        self.data_path = data_path or Path(PathConfig.DATA_DIR) / f\\\"{DataConfig.SYMBOL.lower()}_{DataConfig.INTERVAL}.csv\\\"\\n        self.results_dir = Path(results_dir)\\n        self.log_dir = Path(log_dir)\\n\\n        self.results_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\\n        self.log_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\\n\\n        # Current state\\n        self.df = None\\n        self.feature_names = []\\n        self.main_population = None\\n        self.horizon_ensemble = None\\n        self.consensus_gate = None\\n        self.validator = None\\n        self.trader = None\\n\\n        # Load or initialize\\n        self._init_components()\\n\\n    def _init_components(self):\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"Initialize all system components.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        self.validator = WalkForwardValidator(\\n            train_days=ValidationConfig.TRAIN_DAYS,\\n            valid_days=ValidationConfig.VALID_DAYS,\\n            step_days=ValidationConfig.STEP_DAYS,\\n            min_train_samples=ValidationConfig.MIN_TRAIN_SAMPLES\\n        )\\n\\n        self.trader = bet_size=TradingConfig.POSITION_SIZE\\n        )\\n\\n        self.horizon_ensemble = HorizonEnsemble(horizons=FeatureConfig.HORIZONS)\\n        self.consensus_gate = ConsensusGate(\\n            min_models_agree=ConsensusConfig.MIN_MODELS_AGREE,\\n            min_confidence=ConsensusConfig.MIN_CONFIDENCE\\n        )\\n\\n    def load_or_fetch_data(self, force_refetch: bool = False) -> pd.DataFrame:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        Load data from disk or fetch from Binance if not available/forced.\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        if not force_refetch and self.data_path.exists():\\n            print(f\\\"Loading data from {self.data_path}\\\")\\n            df = pd.read_csv(self.data_path, parse_dates=[\\\"open_time\\\"])\\n        else:\\n            print(\\\"Fetching fresh data from Binance...\\\")\\n            df = fetch_and_save_historical(\\n                symbol=DataConfig.SYMBOL,\\n                interval=DataConfig.INTERVAL,\\n                start_date=DataConfig.START_DATE,\\n                filepath=str(self.data_path)\\n            )\\n\\n        if df.empty:\\n            raise ValueError(\\\"No data available\\\")\\n\\n        print(f\\\"Loaded {len(df)} rows of data\\\", flush=True)\\n        print(f\\\"Date range: {df['open_time'].iloc[0]} to {df['open_time'].iloc[-1]}\\\", flush=True)\\n        return df\\n\\n    def prepare_features(self, df: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        Prepare feature matrix X and target y.\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        print(\\\"Preparing features...\\\", flush=True)\\n\\n        # Clean data\\n        df = clean_data(df)\\n\\n        # Create target (next 15-minute direction)\\n        df = create_target(df, forward_periods=1)\\n\\n        # Generate horizon ensemble features (these will be added as extra columns)\\n        print(\\\"  Generating horizon features...\\\", flush=True)\\n        horizon_features = generate_horizon_features(df, FeatureConfig.HORIZONS)\\n\\n        # Merge horizon features into df\\n        df = df.merge(horizon_features, on=\\\"open_time\\\", how=\\\"left\\\")\\n\\n        # Drop rows with NaN in target\\n        df = df.dropna(subset=[\\\"target\\\"])\\n\\n        # Forward fill any remaining NaNs\\n        df = df.ffill().dropna()\\n\\n        print(f\\\"Prepared {len(df)} samples with {len(df.columns)} columns\\\", flush=True)\\n        self.feature_names = [\\n            col for col in df.columns\\n            if col not in [\\n                \\\"open_time\\\", \\\"open\\\", \\\"high\\\", \\\"low\\\", \\\"close\\\", \\\"volume\\\",\\n                \\\"quote_volume\\\", \\\"trades_count\\\", \\\"future_close\\\", \\\"target\\\"\\n            ]\\n        ]\\n\\n        print(f\\\"Feature count: {len(self.feature_names)}\\\", flush=True)\\n        return df\\n\\n    def evolve_population(\\n        self,\\n        X_train: pd.DataFrame,\\n        y_train: pd.Series,\\n        generations: int = ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION\\n    ) -> Population:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        Evolve a population of models.\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        print(f\\\"Evolving population (input_dim={X_train.shape[1]})...\\\", flush=True)\\n\\n        pop = Population(\\n            population_size=ModelConfig.POPULATION_SIZE,\\n            model_types=ModelConfig.MODEL_TYPES,\\n            mutation_rate=ModelConfig.MUTATION_RATE,\\n            crossover_rate=ModelConfig.CROSSOVER_RATE,\\n            elitism_count=ModelConfig.ELITISM_COUNT\\n        )\\n\\n        pop.initialize(input_dim=X_train.shape[1], feature_names=self.feature_names)\\n\\n        for gen in range(generations):\\n            print(f\\\"  Generation {gen + 1}/{generations}\\\", flush=True)\\n\\n            # Evaluate all individuals\\n            for i, ind in enumerate(pop.individuals):\\n                try:\\n                    print(f\\\"    Training individual {i+1}/{len(pop.individuals)} ({ind.model_type})\\\", flush=True)\\n                    ind.model.fit(X_train, y_train)\\n                    proba = ind.model.predict_proba(X_train)\\n                    pred = ind.model.predict(X_train, threshold=0.5)\\n                    accuracy = (pred == y_train).mean()\\n\\n                    # Simple fitness based on training accuracy (we'll validate on walk-forward later)\\n                    ind.fitness = accuracy\\n                    print(f\\\"      -> fitness={ind.fitness:.4f}\\\", flush=True)\\n                except Exception as e:\\n                    print(f\\\"      -> training error: {e}\\\", flush=True)\\n                    ind.fitness = -1000.0\\n\\n            # Evolve to next generation, but not after the final generation\\n            if gen < generations - 1:\\n                print(\\\"  Evolving to next generation...\\\", flush=True)\\n                pop.evolve()\\n\\n            best = pop.get_best(1)[0]\\n            print(f\\\"    Best fitness: {best.fitness:.4f} (model={best.model_type})\\\", flush=True)\\n\\n        return pop\\n\\n    def run_walk_forward(\\n        self,\\n        df: pd.DataFrame\\n    ) -> Dict[str, float]:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        Run walk-forward validation with a single temporal split.\\n        Train on data before the validation period, test on the most recent validation period.\\n        This provides an honest OOS performance estimate without leakage.\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        print(\\\"Running walk-forward validation (single split)...\\\", flush=True)\\n\\n        df_sorted = df.sort_values(\\\"open_time\\\").reset_index(drop=True)\\n\\n        # Determine validation period length (e.g., 7 days)\\n        val_days = ValidationConfig.VALID_DAYS\\n        val_rows = int(val_days * 24 * 60 / 15)  # number of 15-min candles\\n\\n        total_rows = len(df_sorted)\\n        if total_rows < val_rows + 1000:\\n            print(\\\"Insufficient data for walk-forward split.\\\", flush=True)\\n            return {\\\"accuracy\\\": 0.0, \\\"bets_per_month\\\": 0.0, \\\"n_folds\\\": 0}\\n\\n        # Split: validation = most recent val_rows, training = older data\\n        valid_df = df_sorted.iloc[-val_rows:].copy()\\n        train_df = df_sorted.iloc[:-val_rows].copy()\\n\\n        # Limit training samples to most recent MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES (controls memory/time)\\n        if len(train_df) > ModelConfig.MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES:\\n            train_df = train_df.tail(ModelConfig.MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES).copy()\\n            print(f\\\"Limited training to {len(train_df)} most recent samples\\\", flush=True)\\n\\n        print(f\\\"Train: {len(train_df)} samples, Validation: {len(valid_df)} samples\\\", flush=True)\\n\\n        X_train = train_df[self.feature_names]\\n        y_train = train_df[\\\"target\\\"]\\n        X_valid = valid_df[self.feature_names]\\n        y_valid = valid_df[\\\"target\\\"]\\n\\n        # Evolve a fresh population on the training set\\n        pop = Population(\\n            population_size=ModelConfig.POPULATION_SIZE,\\n            model_types=ModelConfig.MODEL_TYPES,\\n            mutation_rate=ModelConfig.MUTATION_RATE,\\n            crossover_rate=ModelConfig.CROSSOVER_RATE,\\n            elitism_count=ModelConfig.ELITISM_COUNT\\n        )\\n        pop.initialize(input_dim=X_train.shape[1], feature_names=self.feature_names)\\n\\n        for gen in range(ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION):\\n            print(f\\\"  Generation {gen+1}/{ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION}\\\", flush=True)\\n            # Evaluate individuals on training data\\n            for ind in pop.individuals:\\n                try:\\n                    ind.model.fit(X_train, y_train)\\n                    # Use training accuracy as fitness for selection\\n                    pred = ind.model.predict(X_train, threshold=0.5)\\n                    ind.fitness = (pred == y_train).mean()\\n                except Exception as e:\\n                    print(f\\\"    Training error: {e}\\\", flush=True)\\n                    ind.fitness = -1000.0\\n\\n            pop.evolve()\\n            best = pop.get_best(1)[0]\\n            print(f\\\"    Best fitness: {best.fitness:.4f} ({best.model_type})\\\", flush=True)\\n\\n        # Prepare close prices for simulation (used later and here)\\n        close_prices = valid_df[\\\"close\\\"]\\n\\n        # Re-score individuals on validation set using Sharpe (profitability)\\n        print(\\\"Re-scoring population on validation set (Sharpe-based)...\\\", flush=True)\\n        for i, ind in enumerate(pop.individuals):\\n            # Skip individuals that failed training\\n            if ind.fitness is None or ind.fitness <= -1000:\\n                print(f\\\"  Skipping individual {i+1}/{len(pop.individuals)} (failed training)\\\", flush=True)\\n                continue\\n            try:\\n                print(f\\\"  Rescoring individual {i+1}/{len(pop.individuals)} ({ind.model_type})\\\", flush=True)\\n                # Predict on validation\\n                pred_valid = ind.model.predict(X_valid, threshold=0.5)\\n                # Ensure pred_valid is a Series with index\\n                if isinstance(pred_valid, np.ndarray):\\n                    pred_valid = pd.Series(pred_valid, index=X_valid.index)\\n                # Create signals series aligned to close_prices (fill missing with -1)\\n                signals_ind = pred_valid.reindex(close_prices.index).fillna(-1).astype(int)\\n                # Run simulation\\n                self.trader.run(signals_ind, close_prices)\\n                v_metrics = self.trader.metrics\\n                # Use Sharpe as fitness (higher is better)\\n                ind.fitness = v_metrics.get('sharpe', -1000)\\n                print(f\\\"    -> Sharpe: {ind.fitness:.4f}\\\", flush=True)\\n                # Preserve validation metrics for later analysis if needed\\n                ind.validation_metrics = v_metrics\\n            except Exception as e:\\n                print(f\\\"  Validation rescoring error: {e}\\\", flush=True)\\n                ind.fitness = -1000.0\\n\\n        # Get top diverse models based on validation Sharpe\\n        top_models = pop.get_diverse_best(n=5)\\n\\n        # Generate consensus signals on validation set\\n        consensus = ConsensusGate(\\n            min_models_agree=ConsensusConfig.MIN_MODELS_AGREE,\\n            min_confidence=ConsensusConfig.MIN_CONFIDENCE\\n        )\\n        signals = consensus.decide(top_models, X_valid)\\n\\n        # Align close prices (indices correspond)\\n        close_prices = valid_df[\\\"close\\\"]\\n\\n        # Run simulator on signals\\n        self.trader.run(signals, close_prices)\\n        metrics = self.trader.metrics.copy()\\n\\n        # Compute raw accuracy\\n        mask = (signals != -1) & y_valid.notna()\\n        if mask.any():\\n            metrics[\\\"accuracy\\\"] = (signals[mask] == y_valid[mask]).mean()\\n        else:\\n            metrics[\\\"accuracy\\\"] = 0.0\\n\\n        metrics[\\\"n_folds\\\"] = 1\\n        print(f\\\"Walk-forward results: accuracy={metrics['accuracy']:.4f}, bets/month={metrics.get('bets_per_month', 0):.2f}, Sharpe={metrics.get('sharpe', 0):.4f}\\\", flush=True)\\n\\n        return metrics\\n\\n    def train_best_models(\\n        self,\\n        df: pd.DataFrame,\\n        population: Population,\\n        horizon_data: dict = None\\n    ):\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        Train the best models on full recent data and optionally train horizon ensembles.\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        print(\\\"Training best models on full data...\\\", flush=True)\\n\\n        # Use recent data (last N days) for final training\\n        recent_days = 60\\n        cutoff_time = df[\\\"open_time\\\"].max() - timedelta(days=recent_days)\\n        recent_df = df[df[\\\"open_time\\\"] >= cutoff_time].copy()\\n\\n        X_recent = recent_df[self.feature_names]\\n        y_recent = recent_df[\\\"target\\\"]\\n\\n        # Get top models\\n        top_models = population.get_diverse_best(n=ConsensusConfig.MIN_MODELS_AGREE + 2)\\n\\n        # Retrain on full recent data\\n        for ind in top_models:\\n            try:\\n validation with a single temporal split.\\n   204→        Train on data before the validation period, test on the most recent validation period.\\n   205→        This provides an honest OOS performance estimate without leakage.\\n   206→        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   207→        print(\\\"Running walk-forward validation (single split)...\\\", flush=True)\\n   208→\\n   209→        df_sorted = df.sort_values(\\\"open_time\\\").reset_index(drop=True)\\n   210→\\n   211→        # Determine validation period length (e.g., 7 days)\\n   212→        val_days = ValidationConfig.VALID_DAYS\\n   213→        val_rows = int(val_days * 24 * 60 / 15)  # number of 15-min candles\\n   214→\\n   215→        total_rows = len(df_sorted)\\n   216→        if total_rows < val_rows + 1000:\\n   217→            print(\\\"Insufficient data for walk-forward split.\\\", flush=True)\\n   218→            return {\\\"accuracy\\\": 0.0, \\\"bets_per_month\\\": 0.0, \\\"n_folds\\\": 0}\\n   219→\\n   220→        # Split: validation = most recent val_rows, training = older data\\n   221→        valid_df = df_sorted.iloc[-val_rows:].copy()\\n   222→        train_df = df_sorted.iloc[:-val_rows].copy()\\n   223→\\n   224→        # Limit training samples to most recent MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES (controls memory/time)\\n   225→        if len(train_df) > ModelConfig.MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES:\\n   226→            train_df = train_df.tail(ModelConfig.MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES).copy()\\n   227→            print(f\\\"Limited training to {len(train_df)} most recent samples\\\", flush=True)\\n   228→\\n   229→        print(f\\\"Train: {len(train_df)} samples, Validation: {len(valid_df)} samples\\\", flush=True)\\n   230→\\n   231→        X_train = train_df[self.feature_names]\\n   232→        y_train = train_df[\\\"target\\\"]\\n   233→        X_valid = valid_df[self.feature_names]\\n   234→        y_valid = valid_df[\\\"target\\\"]\\n   235→\\n   236→        # Evolve a fresh population on the training set\\n   237→        pop = Population(\\n   238→            population_size=ModelConfig.POPULATION_SIZE,\\n   239→            model_types=ModelConfig.MODEL_TYPES,\\n   240→            mutation_rate=ModelConfig.MUTATION_RATE,\\n   241→            crossover_rate=ModelConfig.CROSSOVER_RATE,\\n   242→            elitism_count=ModelConfig.ELITISM_COUNT\\n   243→        )\\n   244→        pop.initialize(input_dim=X_train.shape[1], feature_names=self.feature_names)\\n   245→\\n   246→        for gen in range(ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION):\\n   247→            print(f\\\"  Generation {gen+1}/{ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION}\\\", flush=True)\\n   248→            # Evaluate individuals on training data\\n   249→            for ind in pop.individuals:\\n   250→                try:\\n   251→                    ind.model.fit(X_train, y_train)\\n   252→                    # Use training accuracy as fitness for selection\\n   253→                    pred = ind.model.predict(X_train, threshold=0.5)\\n   254→                    ind.fitness = (pred == y_train).mean()\\n   255→                except Exception as e:\\n   256→                    print(f\\\"    Training error: {e}\\\", flush=True)\\n   257→                    ind.fitness = -1000.0\\n   258→\\n   259→            pop.evolve()\\n   260→            best = pop.get_best(1)[0]\\n   261→            print(f\\\"    Best fitness: {best.fitness:.4f} ({best.model_type})\\\", flush=True)\\n   262→\\n   263→        # Prepare close prices for simulation (used later and here)\\n   264→        close_prices = valid_df[\\\"close\\\"]\\n   265→\\n   266→        # Re-score individuals on validation set using Sharpe (profitability)\\n   267→        print(\\\"Re-scoring population on validation set (Sharpe-based)...\\\", flush=True)\\n   268→        for ind in pop.individuals:\\n   269→            # Skip individuals that failed training\\n   270→            if ind.fitness is None or ind.fitness <= -1000:\\n   271→                continue\\n   272→            try:\\n   273→                # Predict on validation\\n   274→                pred_valid = ind.model.predict(X_valid, threshold=0.5)\\n   275→                # Ensure pred_valid is a Series with index\\n   276→                if isinstance(pred_valid, np.ndarray):\\n   277→                    pred_valid = pd.Series(pred_valid, index=X_valid.index)\\n   278→                # Create signals series aligned to close_prices (fill missing with -1)\\n   279→                signals_ind = pred_valid.reindex(close_prices.index).fillna(-1).astype(int)\\n   280→                # Run simulation\\n   281→                self.trader.run(signals_ind, close_prices)\\n   282→                v_metrics = self.trader.metrics\\n   283→                # Use Sharpe as fitness (higher is better)\\n   284→                ind.fitness = v_metrics.get('sharpe', -1000)\\n   285→                # top_models = pop.get_diverse_best(n=5)\\n   293→\\n   294→        # Generate consensus signals on validation set\\n   295→        consensus = ConsensusGate(\\n   296→            min_models_agree=ConsensusConfig.MIN_MODELS_AGREE,\\n   297→            min_confidence=ConsensusConfig.MIN_CONFIDENCE\\n   298→        )\\n   299→        signals = consensus.decide(top_models, accuracy (we'll validate on walk-forward later)\\n   185→                    ind.fitness = accuracy\\n   186→                except Exception as e:\\n   187→                    ind.fitness = 197→\\n   198→    def run_walk_forward(\\n   199→        self,\\n   200→        df: pd.DataFrame\\n   201→    ) -> Dict[str, float]:\\n   202→        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   203→        Run walk-forward validation with a single temporal split.\\n   204→        Train on data before the validation period, test on the most recent validation period.\\n   205→        This provides an honest OOS performance estimate without leakage.\\n   206→        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   207→        print(\\\"Running walk-forward validation (single split)...\\\", flush=True)\\n   208→\\n   209→        df_sorted = 213→        val_rows = int(val_days * 24 * 60 / 15)  # number of 15-min candles\\n   214→\\n   215→        total_rows = len(df_sorted)\\n   216→        if total_rows < val_rows + 1000:\\n   217→            print(\\\"Insufficient data for walk-forward split.\\\", flush=True)\\n   218→            return {\\\"accuracy\\\": 0.0, \\\"bets_per_month\\\": 0.0, \\\"n_folds\\\": 0}\\n   219→\\n   220→ 223→\\n   224→        # Limit training samples to most recent MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES (controls memory/time)\\n   225→        if len(train_df) > ModelConfig.MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES:\\n   226→            train_df = train_df.tail(ModelConfig.MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES).copy()\\n   227→            print(f\\\"Limited training to {len(train_df)} most recent samples\\\", flush=True)\\n   228→\\n   229→        print(f\\\"Train: {len(train_df)} samples, Validation: {len(valid_df)} samples\\\", flush=True)\\n   230→\\n   231→        X_train = train_df[self.feature_names]\\n   232→        y_train = train_df[\\\"target\\\"]\\n   233→        X_valid = valid_df[self.feature_names]\\n   234→        y_valid = valid_df[\\\"target\\\"]\\n   235→\\n   236→        # Evolve a fresh population on the training set\\n   237→        pop = Population(\\n   238→            population_size=ModelConfig.POPULATION_SIZE,\\n   239→            model_types=ModelConfig.MODEL_TYPES,\\n   240→            mutation_rate=ModelConfig.MUTATION_RATE,\\n   241→            crossover_rate=ModelConfig.CROSSOVER_RATE,\\n   242→            elitism_count=ModelConfig.ELITISM_COUNT\\n   243→        )\\n   244→        pop.initialize(input_dim=X_train.shape[1], feature_names=self.feature_names)\\n   245→\\n   246→        for gen in range(ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION):\\n   247→            print(f\\\"  Generation {gen+1}/{ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION}\\\", flush=True)\\n   248→            # Evaluate individuals on training data\\n   249→            for ind in pop.individuals:\\n   250→                try:\\n   251→                    ind.model.fit(X_train, y_train)\\n   252→                    # Use training accuracy as fitness for selection\\n   253→                    pred = ind.model.predict(X_train, threshold=0.5)\\n   254→                    ind.fitness = (pred == y_train).mean()\\n   255→                except Exception as e:\\n   256→                    print(f\\\"    Training error: {e}\\\", flush=True)\\n   257→                    ind.fitness = -1000.0\\n   258→\\n   259→            pop.evolve()\\n   260→            best = pop.get_best(1)[0]\\n   261→            print(f\\\"    Best fitness: {best.fitness:.4f} ({best.model_type})\\\", flush=True)\\n   262→\\n   263→        # Prepare close prices for simulation (used later and here)\\n   264→        close_prices = valid_df[\\\"close\\\"]\\n   265→\\n   266→        # Re-score individuals on validation set using Sharpe (profitability)\\n   267→        print(\\\"Re-scoring population on validation set (Sharpe-based)...\\\", flush=True)\\n   268→        for ind in pop.individuals:\\n   269→            # Skip individuals that failed training\\n   270→            if ind.fitness is None or ind.fitness <= -1000:\\n   271→                continue\\n   272→            try:\\n   273→                # Predict on validation\\n   274→                pred_valid = ind.model.predict(X_valid, threshold=0.5)\\n   275→                # Ensure pred_valid is a Series with index\\n   276→                if isinstance(pred_valid, np.ndarray):\\n   277→                    pred_valid = pd.Series(pred_valid, index=X_valid.index)\\n   278→                # Create signals series aligned to close_prices (fill missing with -1)\\n   279→                signals_ind = pred_valid.reindex(close_prices.index).fillna(-1).astype(int)\\n   280→                # Run simulation\\n   281→                self.trader.run(signals_ind, close_prices)\\n   282→                v_metrics = self.trader.metrics\\n   283→                # Use Sharpe as fitness (higher is better)\\n   284→                ind.fitness = v_metrics.get('sharpe', -1000)\\n   285→                # Preserve validation metrics for later analysis if needed\\n   286→                ind.validation_metrics = v_metrics\\n   287→            except Exception as e:\\n   288→                print(f\\\"  Validation rescoring error: {e}\\\", flush=True)\\n   289→                ind.fitness = -1000.0\\n   290→\\n   291→        # Get top diverse models based on validation Sharpe\\n   292→        top_models = pop.get_diverse_best(n=5)\\n   293→\\n   294→        # Generate consensus signals on validation set\\n   295→        consensus = ConsensusGate(\\n   296→            min_models_agree=ConsensusConfig.MIN_MODELS_AGREE,\\n   297→            min_confidence=ConsensusConfig.MIN_CONFIDENCE\\n   298→        )\\n   299→        signals = consensus.decide(top_models, X_valid)\\n   300→\\n   301→        # Align close prices (indices correspond)\\n   302→        close_prices = valid_df[\\\"close\\\"]\\n   303→\\n   304→        # Run simulator on signals\\n   305→        self.trader.run(signals, close_prices)\\n   306→        metrics = self.trader.metrics.copy()\\n   307→\\n   308→        # Compute raw accuracy\\n   309→        mask = (signals != -1) & y_valid.notna()\\n   310→        if mask.any():\\n   311→            metrics[\\\"accuracy\\\"] = (signals[mask] == y_valid[mask]).mean()\\n   312→        else:\\n   313→            metrics[\\\"accuracy\\\"] = 0.0\\n   314→\\n   315→        metrics[\\\"n_folds\\\"] = 1\\n   316→        print(f\\\"Walk-forward results: accuracy={metrics['accuracy']:.4f}, bets/month={metrics.get('bets_per_month', 0):.2f}, Sharpe={metrics.get('sharpe', 0):.4f}\\\", flush=True)\\n   317→\\n   318→        return metrics\\n   319→\\n   320→    def train_best_models(\\n   321→        self,\\n   322→        df: pd.DataFrame,\\n   323→        population: Population,\\n   324→        horizon_data: dict = None\\n   325→    ):\\n   326→        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   327→        Train the best models on full recent data and optionally train horizon ensembles.\\n   328→        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   329→        print(\\\"Training best models on full data...\\\", flush=True)\\n   330→\\n   331→        # Use recent data (last N days) for final training\\n   332→        recent_days = 60\\n   333→        cutoff_time = df[\\\"open_time\\\"].max() - timedelta(days=recent_days)\\n   334→        recent_df = df[df[\\\"open_time\\\"] >= cutoff_time].copy()\\n   335→\\n   336→        X_recent = recent_df[self.feature_names]\\n   337→        y_recent = recent_df[\\\"target\\\"]\\n   338→\\n   339→        # Get top models\\n   340→        top_models = population.get_diverse_best(n=ConsensusConfig.MIN_MODELS_AGREE + 2)\\n   341→\\n   342→        # Retrain on full recent data\\n   343→        for ind in top_models:\\n   344→            try:\\n   345→                ind.model.fit(X_recent, y_recent)\\n   346→            except Exception as e:\\n   347→                print(f\\\"  Failed to train {ind.model_type}: {e}\\\", flush=True)\\n   348→\\n   349→        # Train horizon ensemble if not provided\\n   350→        if horizon_data is None:\\n   351→            print(\\\"Training horizon ensemble...\\\", flush=True)\\n   352→            horizon_data = {}\\n   353→            for h_name, factor in FeatureConfig.HORIZONS.items():\\n   354→                if h_name == \\\"15m\\\":\\n   355→                    continue\\n   356→                # Resample and prepare features for this horizon\\n   357→                from .data.processor import resample_to_horizon\\n   358→                df_horizon = resample_to_horizon(recent_df, recent_days, factor)\\n   359→                if len(df_horizon) > 100:\\n   360→                    df_horizon = create_target(df_horizon, forward_periods=1)\\n   361→                    df_horizon = add_technical_indicators(df_horizon)\\n   362→                    df_horizon = add_regime_features(df_horizon)\\n   363→                    df_horizon = df_horizon.dropna()\\n   364→                    horizon_data[h_name] = df_horizon\\n   365→\\n   366→        self.horizon_ensemble.fit_horizon_models(horizon_data, y_recent)\\n   367→\\n   368→        self.main_population = population\\n   369→        self.top_models = top_models\\n   370→\\n   371→    def \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   377→        Generate trading signals for the most recent data points.\\n   378→        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n   379→        if self.top_models is None:\\n   380→            raise Load/fetch latest data\\n   396→        if new_data is None:\\n   397→            df = self.load_or_fetch_data()\\n   398→        else:\\n   399→            df = new_data\\n   400→\\n   401→        # 2. For online operation, limit to most recent N days to bound runtime\\n   402→        if ModelConfig.ONLINE_DATA_DAYS is not None:\\n   403→            cutoff = df[\\\"open_time\\\"].max() - timedelta(days=ModelConfig.ONLINE_DATA_DAYS)\\n   404→            df = df[df[\\\"open_time\\\"] >= cutoff].copy()\\n   405→            print(f\\\"Online mode: limited data to last {ModelConfig.ONLINE_DATA_DAYS} days ({len(df)} rows)\\\", flush=True)\\n   406→\\n   407→        # 3. Prepare features\\n   408→        df = self.prepare_features(df)\\n   409→\\n   410→        # 3. Split into recent data for training and current for prediction\\n   411→        # Use last 30 days for training, current for signal generation\\n   412→        recent_cutoff = df[\\\"open_time\\\"].max() - timedelta(days=30)\\n   413→        train_df = df[df[\\\"open_time\\\"] <= recent_cutoff].copy()\\n   414→        current_df = df[df[\\\"open_time\\\"] > recent_cutoff].copy()\\n   415→\\n   416→        # Limit training samples to control memory/time (use most recent samples)\\n   417→        if len(train_df) > ModelConfig.MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES:\\n   418→            train_df = train_df.tail(ModelConfig.MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES).copy()\\n   419→            print(f\\\"Limited training to {len(train_df)} most recent samples\\\", flush=True)\\n   420→\\n   421→        if len(train_df) < ValidationConfig.MIN_TRAIN_SAMPLES:\\n   422→            print(f\\\"Insufficient training data: {len(train_df)} samples\\\", flush=True)\\n   423→            return None\\n   424→\\n   425→        X_train = train_df[self.feature_names]\\n   426→        y_train = train_df[\\\"target\\\"]\\n   427→\\n   428→        # 4. Evolve population\\n   429→        population = self.evolve_population(X_train, y_train, generations=ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION)\\n   430→\\n   431→        # 5. Walk-forward validation on historical folds (use recent data to limit folds)\\n   432→        if ValidationConfig.MAX_WALK_FORWARD_DAYS is not None:\\n   433→            wf_cutoff = df[\\\"open_time\\\"].max() - timedelta(days=ValidationConfig.MAX_WALK_FORWARD_DAYS)\\n   434→            df_wf = df[df[\\\"open_time\\\"] >= wf_cutoff].copy()\\n   435→            print(f\\\"Using {len(df_wf)} recent samples for walk-forward validation (max {ValidationConfig.MAX_WALK_FORWARD_DAYS} days)\\\", flush=True)\\n   436→        else:\\n   437→            df_wf = df\\n   438→        metrics = self.run_walk_forward(df_wf)\\n   439→\\n   440→        # 6. train_days=ValidationConfig.TRAIN_DAYS,\n            valid_days=ValidationConfig.VALID_DAYS,\n            step_days=ValidationConfig.STEP_DAYS,\n            min_train_samples=ValidationConfig.MIN_TRAIN_SAMPLES\n        )\n\n        self.trader = SimulatedTrader(\n            initial_capital=TradingConfig.INITIAL_CAPITAL,\n            fee_rate=TradingConfig.FEE_RATE,\n            slippage=TradingConfig.SLIPPAGE,\n            bet_size=TradingConfig.POSITION_SIZE\n        )\n\n        self.horizon_ensemble = HorizonEnsemble(horizons=FeatureConfig.HORIZONS)\n        self.consensus_gate = ConsensusGate(\n            min_models_agree=ConsensusConfig.MIN_MODELS_AGREE,\n            min_confidence=ConsensusConfig.MIN_CONFIDENCE\n        )\n\n    def load_or_fetch_data(self, force_refetch: bool = False) -> pd.DataFrame:\n        \"\"\"\n        Load data from disk or fetch from Binance if not available/forced.\n        \"\"\"\n        if not force_refetch and self.data_path.exists():\n            print(f\"Loading data from {self.data_path}\")\n            df = pd.read_csv(self.data_path, parse_dates=[\"open_time\"])\n        else:\n            print(\"Fetching fresh data from Binance...\")\n            df = fetch_and_save_historical(\n                symbol=DataConfig.SYMBOL,\n                interval=DataConfig.INTERVAL,\n                start_date=DataConfig.START_DATE,\n                filepath=str(self.data_path)\n            )\n\n        if df.empty:\n            raise ValueError(\"No data available\")\n\n        print(f\"Loaded {len(df)} rows of data\", flush=True)\n        print(f\"Date range: {df['open_time'].iloc[0]} to {df['open_time'].iloc[-1]}\", flush=True)\n        return df\n\n    def prepare_features(self, df: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:\n        \"\"\"\n        Prepare feature matrix X and target y.\n        \"\"\"\n        print(\"Preparing features...\", flush=True)\n\n        # Clean data\n        df = clean_data(df)\n\n        # Create target (next 15-minute direction)\n        df = create_target(df, forward_periods=1)\n\n        # Generate horizon ensemble features (these will be added as extra columns)\n        print(\"  Generating horizon features...\", flush=True)\n        horizon_features = generate_horizon_features(df, FeatureConfig.HORIZONS)\n\n        # Merge horizon features into df\n        df = df.merge(horizon_features, on=\"open_time\", how=\"left\")\n\n        # Drop rows with NaN in target\n        df = df.dropna(subset=[\"target\"])\n\n        # Forward fill any remaining NaNs\n        df = df.ffill().dropna()\n\n        print(f\"Prepared {len(df)} samples with {len(df.columns)} columns\", flush=True)\n        self.feature_names = [\n            col for col in df.columns\n            if col not in [\n                \"open_time\", \"open\", \"high\", \"low\", \"close\", \"volume\",\n                \"quote_volume\", \"trades_count\", \"future_close\", \"target\"\n            ]\n        ]\n\n        print(f\"Feature count: {len(self.feature_names)}\", flush=True)\n        return df\n\n    def evolve_population(\n        self,\n        X_train: pd.DataFrame,\n        y_train: pd.Series,\n        generations: int = ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION\n    ) -> Population:\n        \"\"\"\n        Evolve a population of models.\n        \"\"\"\n        print(f\"Evolving population (input_dim={X_train.shape[1]})...\", flush=True)\n\n        pop = Population(\n            population_size=ModelConfig.POPULATION_SIZE,\n            model_types=ModelConfig.MODEL_TYPES,\n            mutation_rate=ModelConfig.MUTATION_RATE,\n            crossover_rate=ModelConfig.CROSSOVER_RATE,\n            elitism_count=ModelConfig.ELITISM_COUNT\n        )\n\n        pop.initialize(input_dim=X_train.shape[1], feature_names=self.feature_names)\n\n        for gen in range(generations):\n            print(f\"  Generation {gen + 1}/{generations}\", flush=True)\n\n            # Evaluate all individuals\n            for i, ind in enumerate(pop.individuals):\n                try:\n                    print(f\"    Training individual {i+1}/{len(pop.individuals)} ({ind.model_type})\", flush=True)\n                    ind.model.fit(X_train, y_train)\n                    proba = ind.model.predict_proba(X_train)\n                    pred = ind.model.predict(X_train, threshold=0.5)\n                    accuracy = (pred == y_train).mean()\n\n                    # Simple fitness based on training accuracy (we'll validate on walk-forward later)\n                    ind.fitness = accuracy\n                    print(f\"      -> fitness={ind.fitness:.4f}\", flush=True)\n                except Exception as e:\n                    print(f\"      -> training error: {e}\", flush=True)\n                    ind.fitness = -1000.0\n\n            # Evolve to next generation, but not after the final generation\n            if gen < generations - 1:\n                print(\"  Evolving to next generation...\", flush=True)\n                pop.evolve()\n\n            best = pop.get_best(1)[0]\n            print(f\"    Best fitness: {best.fitness:.4f} (model={best.model_type})\", flush=True)\n\n        return pop\n\n    def run_walk_forward(\n        self,\n        df: pd.DataFrame\n    ) -> Dict[str, float]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Run walk-forward validation with a single temporal split.\n        Train on data before the validation period, test on the most recent validation period.\n        This provides an honest OOS performance estimate without leakage.\n        \"\"\"\n        print(\"Running walk-forward validation (single split)...\", flush=True)\n\n        df_sorted = df.sort_values(\"open_time\").reset_index(drop=True)\n\n        # Determine validation period length (e.g., 7 days)\n        val_days = ValidationConfig.VALID_DAYS\n        val_rows = int(val_days * 24 * 60 / 15)  # number of 15-min candles\n\n        total_rows = len(df_sorted)\n        if total_rows < val_rows + 1000:\n            print(\"Insufficient data for walk-forward split.\", flush=True)\n            return {\"accuracy\": 0.0, \"bets_per_month\": 0.0, \"n_folds\": 0}\n\n        # Split: validation = most recent val_rows, training = older data\n        valid_df = df_sorted.iloc[-val_rows:].copy()\n        train_df = df_sorted.iloc[:-val_rows].copy()\n\n        # Limit training samples to most recent MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES (controls memory/time)\n        if len(train_df) > ModelConfig.MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES:\n            train_df = train_df.tail(ModelConfig.MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES).copy()\n            print(f\"Limited training to {len(train_df)} most recent samples\", flush=True)\n\n        print(f\"Train: {len(train_df)} samples, Validation: {len(valid_df)} samples\", flush=True)\n\n        X_train = train_df[self.feature_names]\n        y_train = train_df[\"target\"]\n        X_valid = valid_df[self.feature_names]\n        y_valid = valid_df[\"target\"]\n\n        # Evolve a fresh population on the training set\n        pop = Population(\n            population_size=ModelConfig.POPULATION_SIZE,\n            model_types=ModelConfig.MODEL_TYPES,\n            mutation_rate=ModelConfig.MUTATION_RATE,\n            crossover_rate=ModelConfig.CROSSOVER_RATE,\n            elitism_count=ModelConfig.ELITISM_COUNT\n        )\n        pop.initialize(input_dim=X_train.shape[1], feature_names=self.feature_names)\n\n        for gen in range(ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION):\n            print(f\"  Generation {gen+1}/{ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION}\", flush=True)\n            # Evaluate individuals on training data\n            for ind in pop.individuals:\n                try:\n                    ind.model.fit(X_train, y_train)\n                    # Use training accuracy as fitness for selection\n                    pred = ind.model.predict(X_train, threshold=0.5)\n                    ind.fitness = (pred == y_train).mean()\n                except Exception as e:\n                    print(f\"    Training error: {e}\", flush=True)\n                    ind.fitness = -1000.0\n\n            pop.evolve()\n            best = pop.get_best(1)[0]\n            print(f\"    Best fitness: {best.fitness:.4f} ({best.model_type})\", flush=True)\n\n        # Prepare close prices for simulation (used later and here)\n        close_prices = valid_df[\"close\"]\n\n        # Re-score individuals on validation set using Sharpe (profitability)\n        print(\"Re-scoring population on validation set (Sharpe-based)...\", flush=True)\n        for i, ind in enumerate(pop.individuals):\n            # Skip individuals that failed training\n            if ind.fitness is None or ind.fitness <= -1000:\n                print(f\"  Skipping individual {i+1}/{len(pop.individuals)} (failed training)\", flush=True)\n                continue\n            try:\n                print(f\"  Rescoring individual {i+1}/{len(pop.individuals)} ({ind.model_type})\", flush=True)\n                # Predict on validation\n                pred_valid = ind.model.predict(X_valid, threshold=0.5)\n                # Ensure pred_valid is a Series with index\n                if isinstance(pred_valid, np.ndarray):\n                    pred_valid = pd.Series(pred_valid, index=X_valid.index)\n                # Create signals series aligned to close_prices (fill missing with -1)\n                signals_ind = pred_valid.reindex(close_prices.index).fillna(-1).astype(int)\n                # Run simulation\n                self.trader.run(signals_ind, close_prices)\n                v_metrics = self.trader.metrics\n                # Use Sharpe as fitness (higher is better)\n                ind.fitness = v_metrics.get('sharpe', -1000)\n                print(f\"    -> Sharpe: {ind.fitness:.4f}\", flush=True)\n                # Preserve validation metrics for later analysis if needed\n                ind.validation_metrics = v_metrics\n            except Exception as e:\n                print(f\"  Validation rescoring error: {e}\", flush=True)\n                ind.fitness = -1000.0\n\n        # Get top diverse models based on validation Sharpe\n        top_models = pop.get_diverse_best(n=5)\n\n        # Generate consensus signals on validation set\n        consensus = ConsensusGate(\n            min_models_agree=ConsensusConfig.MIN_MODELS_AGREE,\n            min_confidence=ConsensusConfig.MIN_CONFIDENCE\n        )\n        signals = consensus.decide(top_models, X_valid)\n\n        # Align close prices (indices correspond)\n        close_prices = valid_df[\"close\"]\n\n        # Run simulator on signals\n        self.trader.run(signals, close_prices)\n        metrics = self.trader.metrics.copy()\n\n        # Compute raw accuracy\n        mask = (signals != -1) & y_valid.notna()\n        if mask.any():\n            metrics[\"accuracy\"] = (signals[mask] == y_valid[mask]).mean()\n        else:\n            metrics[\"accuracy\"] = 0.0\n\n        metrics[\"n_folds\"] = 1\n        print(f\"Walk-forward results: accuracy={metrics['accuracy']:.4f}, bets/month={metrics.get('bets_per_month', 0):.2f}, Sharpe={metrics.get('sharpe', 0):.4f}\", flush=True)\n\n        return metrics\n\n    def train_best_models(\n        self,\n        df: pd.DataFrame,\n        population: Population,\n        horizon_data: dict = None\n    ):\n        \"\"\"\n        Train the best models on full recent data and optionally train horizon ensembles.\n        \"\"\"\n        print(\"Training best models on full data...\", flush=True)\n\n        # Use recent data (last N days) for final training\n        recent_days = 60\n        cutoff_time = df[\"open_time\"].max() - timedelta(days=recent_days)\n        recent_df = df[df[\"open_time\"] >= cutoff_time].copy()\n        print(f\"Recent training data: {len(recent_df)} samples\", flush=True)\n\n        X_recent = recent_df[self.feature_names]\n        y_recent = recent_df[\"target\"]\n\n        # Get top models\n        top_models = population.get_diverse_best(n=ConsensusConfig.MIN_MODELS_AGREE + 2)\n        print(f\"Selected {len(top_models)} top models for retraining\", flush=True)\n\n        # Retrain on full recent data\n        for i, ind in enumerate(top_models):\n            try:\n                print(f\"  Retraining model {i+1}/{len(top_models)} ({ind.model_type})\", flush=True)\n                ind.model.fit(X_recent, y_recent)\n                print(f\"    Done\", flush=True)\n            except Exception as e:\n                print(f\"  Failed to train {ind.model_type}: {e}\", flush=True)\n\n        # Train horizon ensemble if not provided and if we have multiple horizons\n        if horizon_data is None:\n            horizon_data = {}\n            horizons_to_train = [h for h in FeatureConfig.HORIZONS.keys() if h != \"15m\"]\n            if horizons_to_train:\n                print(f\"Training horizon ensemble for {len(horizons_to_train)} horizons...\", flush=True)\n                for h_name in horizons_to_train:\n                    factor = FeatureConfig.HORIZONS[h_name]\n                    print(f\"  Horizon {h_name} (factor={factor})\", flush=True)\n                    from .data.processor import resample_to_horizon\n                    # Use horizon minutes correctly; but for speed we skip due to minimal HORIZONS\n                    df_horizon = resample_to_horizon(recent_df, recent_days, factor)\n                    print(f\"    Resampled to {len(df_horizon)} rows\", flush=True)\n                    if len(df_horizon) > 100:\n                        df_horizon = create_target(df_horizon, forward_periods=1)\n                        df_horizon = add_technical_indicators(df_horizon)\n                        df_horizon = add_regime_features(df_horizon)\n                        df_horizon = df_horizon.dropna()\n                        print(f\"    Prepared {len(df_horizon)} samples with {len(df_horizon.columns)} features\", flush=True)\n                        horizon_data[h_name] = df_horizon\n                self.horizon_ensemble.fit_horizon_models(horizon_data, y_recent)\n                print(\"Horizon self,\n        current_features: pd.DataFrame,\n        use_horizon: bool = True\n    ) -> pd.Series:\n        \"\"\"\n        Generate trading signals for the most recent data points.\n        \"\"\"\n        if self.top_models is None:\n            raise RuntimeError(\"Models not trained. Call train_best_models first.\")\n\n        signals = self.consensus_gate.decide(self.top_models, current_features)\n        return signals\n\n    def run_online_step(self, new_data: pd.DataFrame = None):\n        \"\"\"\n        Execute one iteration of the online evolution loop.\n\n        This is the main step method called repeatedly by Arbos.\n        \"\"\"\n        print(\"=\" * 60, flush=True)\n        print(\"RUNNING ONLINE STEP\", flush=True)\n        print(\"=\" * 60, flush=True)\n\n        # 1. Load/fetch latest data\n        if new_data is None:\n            df = self.load_or_fetch_data()\n        else:\n            df = new_data\n\n        # 2. For online operation, limit to most recent N days to bound runtime\n        if ModelConfig.ONLINE_DATA_DAYS is not None:\n            cutoff = df[\"open_time\"].max() - timedelta(days=ModelConfig.ONLINE_DATA_DAYS)\n            df = df[df[\"open_time\"] >= cutoff].copy()\n            print(f\"Online mode: limited data to last {ModelConfig.ONLINE_DATA_DAYS} days ({len(df)} rows)\", flush=True)\n\n        # 3. Prepare features\n        df = self.prepare_features(df)\n\n        # 3. Split into recent data for training and current for prediction\n        # Use last 30 days for training, current for signal generation\n        recent_cutoff = df[\"open_time\"].max() - timedelta(days=30)\n        train_df = df[df[\"open_time\"] <= recent_cutoff].copy()\n        current_df = df[df[\"open_time\"] > recent_cutoff].copy()\n\n        # Limit training samples to control memory/time (use most recent samples)\n        if len(train_df) > ModelConfig.MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES:\n            train_df = train_df.tail(ModelConfig.MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES).copy()\n            print(f\"Limited training to {len(train_df)} most recent samples\", flush=True)\n\n        if len(train_df) < ValidationConfig.MIN_TRAIN_SAMPLES:\n            print(f\"Insufficient training data: {len(train_df)} samples\", flush=True)\n            return None\n\n        X_train = train_df[self.feature_names]\n        y_train = train_df[\"target\"]\n\n        # 4. Evolve population\n        population = self.evolve_population(X_train, y_train, generations=ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION)\n\n        # 5. Walk-forward validation on historical folds (use recent data to limit folds)\n        if ValidationConfig.MAX_WALK_FORWARD_DAYS is not None:\n            wf_cutoff = df[\"open_time\"].max() - timedelta(days=ValidationConfig.MAX_WALK_FORWARD_DAYS)\n            df_wf = df[df[\"open_time\"] >= wf_cutoff].copy()\n            print(f\"Using {len(df_wf)} recent samples for walk-forward validation (max {ValidationConfig.MAX_WALK_FORWARD_DAYS} days)\", flush=True)\n        else:\n            df_wf = df\n        metrics = self.run_walk_forward(df_wf)\n\n        # 6. Train best models on full recent data and prepare horizon ensemble\n        self.train_best_models(df, population)\n\n        # 7. Generate current signals\n        if len(current_df) > 0:\n            current_features = current_df[self.feature_names]\n            signals = self.generate_current_signals(current_features)\n            print(f\"Generated {len(signals)} current signals\")\n            latest_signal = signals.iloc[-1] if len(signals) > 0 else None\n            print(f\"Latest signal: {latest_signal} (1=UP, 0=DOWN, -1=NO TRADE)\")\n        else:\n            signals = pd.Series()\n            latest_signal = None\n\n        # 8. Save results\n        self._save_results(metrics, population, signals)\n\n        # Compile full result\n        result = {\n            \"timestamp\": datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),\n            \"accuracy\": metrics.get(\"accuracy\", 0.0),\n            \"bets_per_month\": metrics.get(\"bets_per_month\", 0.0),\n            \"sharpe\": metrics.get(\"sharpe\", 0.0),\n            \"max_drawdown\": metrics.get(\"max_drawdown\", 0.0),\n            \"total_return\": metrics.get(\"total_return\", 0.0),\n            \"latest_signal\": int(latest_signal) if latest_signal is not None else None,\n            \"population_fitness\": {str(i): ind.fitness for i, ind in enumerate(population.get_best(5))}\n        }\n\n        print(\"=\" * 60, flush=True)\n        print(\"STEP COMPLETE\", flush=True)\n        print(f\"Metrics: {json.dumps(result, indent=2)}\", flush=True)\n        print(\"=\" * 60, flush=True)\n\n        return result\n\n    def _save_results(self, metrics: dict, population: Population, signals: pd.Series):\n        \"\"\"Save results to disk.\"\"\"\n        timestamp = datetime.utcnow().strftime(\"%Y%m%d_%H%M%S\")\n        run_dir = self.results_dir / timestamp\n        run_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n        # Save metrics\n        with open(run_dir / \"metrics.json\", \"w\") as f:\n            json.dump(metrics, f, indent=2)\n\n        # Save signals\n        signals.to_csv(run_dir / \"signals.csv\")\n\n        # Save top models\n        top_models = population.get_best(5)\n        model_dir = run_dir / \"models\"\n        model_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)\n        for i, ind in enumerate(top_models):\n            try:\n                ind.model.save(str(model_dir / f\"model_{i}_{ind.model_type}.pkl\"))\n            except Exception as e:\n                print(f\"Could not save model {i}: {e}\")\n\n        # Save population metadata\n        pop_data = []\n        for ind in population.individuals[:10]:\n            pop_data.append({\n                \"model_type\": ind.model_type,\n                \"params\": ind.params,\n                \"fitness\": float(ind.fitness)\n            })\n        with open(run_dir / \"population.json\", \"w\") as f:\n            json.dump(pop_data, f, indent=2)\n\n        print(f\"Results saved to {run_dir}\", flush=True)\n\n\ndef main():\n    \"\"\"Entry point for running the trading system.\"\"\"\n    system = TradingSystem()\n\n    # Combined transaction cost: fee + slippage\n        entry_cost = self.fee_rate + self.slippage\n        exit_cost = self.fee_rate + self.slippage\n\n        # Align signals and prices\n        signals = signals.reindex(prices.index).fillna(-1)\n\n        # Build equity curve indexed by signals.index\n        equity = pd.Series(index=signals.index, dtype=float)\n        equity.iloc[0] = capital\n\n        # Iterate through each timestamp, use signal to open a trade that closes at next timestamp\n        for i in range(len(signals) - 1):\n            sig = signals.iloc[i]\n            current_price = prices.iloc[i]\n            next_price = prices.iloc[i+1]\n\n            if sig == -1:\n                # No trade, carry forward capital\n                equity.iloc[i+1] = capital\n                continue\n\n            if sig == 1:  # long\n                entry = current_price * (1 + entry_cost)\n                exit = next_price * (1 - exit_cost)\n                ret = (exit / entry) - 1\n            else:  # sig == 0, short\n                entry = current_price * (1 - entry_cost)\n                exit = next_price * (1 + exit_cost)\n                ret = (entry / exit) - 1\n\n            # Apply bet size\n            capital *= (1 + ret * self.bet_size)\n\n            self.trades.append({\n                \"entry_time\": signals.index[i],\n                \"exit_time\": signals.index[i+1],\n                \"entry_price\": entry,\n                \"exit_price\": exit,\n                \"return\": ret,\n                \"pnl\": capital - self.initial_capital\n            })\n\n            equity.iloc[i+1] = capital\n\n        # Fill any remaining NaNs in equity with last value\n        equity = equity.ffill()\n\n        self.equity_curve = equity\n\n        # Calculate metrics\n        self.metrics = self._compute_metrics()\n\n        return self.metrics\n\n    def _compute_metrics(self) -> Dict[str, float]:\n        \"\"\"Compute metrics from simulation.\"\"\"\n        if not self.trades:\n            return {\n                \"accuracy\": 0.0,\n                \"bets_per_month\": 0.0,\n                \"sharpe\": 0.0,\n                \"max_drawdown\": 0.0,\n                \"total_return\": 0.0,\n                \"n_trades\": 0,\n                \"profit_factor\": 0.0,\n                \"final_equity\": self.initial_capital\n            }\n\n        returns = [t[\"return\"] for t in self.trades]\n        winning = sum(1 for r in returns if r > 0)\n        accuracy = winning / len(returns)\n\n        # Bets per month (entries)\n        n_entries = len(self.trades)\n        if self.equity_curve is not None and len(self.equity_curve) > 0:\n            period_days = len(self.equity_curve) / (24 * 4)  # 15-min candles to days\n        else:\n            period_days = 0\n        months = period_days / 30.44\n        bets_per_month = n_entries / months if months > 0 else 0\n\n        # Sharpe\n        if self.equity_curve is not None:\n            eq_returns = self.equity_curve.pct_change().dropna()\n            if eq_returns.std() > 0:\n                sharpe = (eq_returns.mean() / eq_returns.std()) * np.sqrt(365 * 24 * 4)\n            else:\n                sharpe = 0.0\n\n            # Max drawdown\n            running_max = self.equity_curve.expanding().max()\n            drawdown = (self.equity_curve - running_max) / running_max\n            max_drawdown = abs(drawdown.min())\n        else:\n            sharpe = 0.0\n            max_drawdown = 0.0\n\n        total_return = (self.equity_curve.iloc[-1] - self.initial_capital) / self.initial_capital\n\n        gross_profit = sum(r for r in returns if r > 0)\n        gross_loss = abs(sum(r for r in returns if r < 0))\n        profit_factor = gross_profit / (gross_loss + 1e-10)\n\n        return {\n            \"accuracy\": accuracy,\n            \"bets_per_month\": bets_per_month,\n            \"sharpe\": sharpe,\n            \"max_drawdown\": max_drawdown,\n            \"total_return\": total_return,\n            \"n_trades\": len(self.trades),\n            \"profit_factor\": profit_factor,\n            \"final_equity\": self.equity_curve.iloc[-1] if self.equity_curve is not None else self.initial_capital,\n            \"gross_profit\": gross_profit,\n            \"gross_loss\": gross_loss\n        }\n\n    def get_equity_curve(self) -> pd.Series:\n        return self.equity_curve\n\n    def get_trades(self) -> pd.DataFrame:\n        return pd.DataFrame(self.trades)\n","numLines":174,"startLine":1,"totalLines":174}}}

At least MIN_MODELS_AGREE models must agree on direction\n    - Average confidence must exceed MIN_CONFIDENCE\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        min_models_agree: int = ConsensusConfig.MIN_MODELS_AGREE,\n        min_confidence: float = ConsensusConfig.MIN_CONFIDENCE\n    ):\n        self.min_models_agree = min_models_agree\n        self.min_confidence = min_confidence\n\n    def decide(\n        self,\n        models: List[Individual],\n        X: pd.DataFrame\n    ) -> pd.Series:\n        \"\"\"\n        Generate final trading signals (UP=1, DOWN=0, NO_TRADE=-1).\n\n        Args:\n            models: list of trained model individuals\n            X: feature DataFrame to predict on\n\n        Returns:\n            Series of signals (1, 0, or -1) aligned to X.index\n        \"\"\"\n        signals = pd.Series(index=X.index, data=0)\n        probas_matrix = []\n\n        for ind in models:\n            try:\n                proba = ind.model.predict_proba(X)\n                probas_matrix.append(proba)\n            except Exception as e:\n                continue\n\n        if not probas_matrix:\n            # No model worked, all NO_TRADE\n            signals[:] = -1\n            return signals\n\n        probas_array = np.array(probas_matrix)  # shape: (n_models, n_samples)\n\n        # Average confidence for each sample\n        avg_proba = probas_array.mean(axis=0)\n\n        # Count models predicting UP\n        up_votes = (probas_array >= 0.5).sum(axis=0)\n\n        down_votes = len(probas_matrix) - up_votes\n\n        # Apply gating rules\n        for i, (avg_p, up, down) in enumerate(zip(avg_proba, up_votes, down_votes)):\n            # Check agreement\n            if up >= self.min_models_agree:\n                direction = 1\n                confidence = avg_p\n            elif down >= self.min_models_agree:\n                direction = 0\n                confidence = 1 - avg_p  # confidence in DOWN is 1 - avg_proba\n            else:\n                signals.iloc[i] = -1\n                continue\n\n            # Check confidence threshold\n            if confidence >= self.min_confidence:\n                signals.iloc[i] = direction\n            else:\n                signals.iloc[i] = -1\n\n        return signals\n\n    def adjust_thresholds(\n        self,\n        recent_performance: Dict[str, float],\n        target_accuracy: float = 0.65,\n        target_bets: float = 90.0\n    ):\n        \"\"\"\n        Dynamically adjust consensus thresholds based on recent performance.\n\n        If accuracy is high but bet count too low: lower min_models_agree or min_confidence\n        If accuracy is low: raise thresholds\n        \"\"\"\n        accuracy = recent_performance.get(\"accuracy\", 0.5)\n        bets_per_month = recent_performance.get(\"bets_per_month\", 0.0)\n\n        if accuracy >= target_accuracy and bets_per_month < target_bets:\n            # Too strict, loosen\n            self.min_models_agree = max(1, self.min_models_agree - 1)\n            self.min_confidence = max(0.5, self.min_confidence - 0.02)\n        elif accuracy < target_accuracy:\n            # Too loose, tighten\n            self.min_models_agree = min(5, self.min_models_agree + 1)\n            self.min_confidence = min(0.8, self.min_confidence + 0.02)\n\n        # Clamp to reasonable ranges\n        self.min_models_agree = max(1, min(5, self.min_models_agree))\n        self.min_confidence = max(0.5, min(0.9, self.min_confidence))\n\n    def get_params(self) -> dict:\n        return {\n            \"min_models_agree\": self.min_models_agree,\n            \"min_confidence\": self.min_confidence\n        }\n","numLines":123,"startLine":1,"totalLines":123}}}

Let's check the simulator's trade logic:\n\nFor long: entry = current_price * (1 + entry_cost), exit = next_price * (1 - exit_cost), ret = (exit/entry)-1.\nFor short: entry = current_price * (1 - entry_cost), exit = next_price * (1 + exit_cost), ret = (entry/exit)-1.\n\nThese formulas compute the return after round-turn costs. That seems correct.\n\nBut note: the bet_size is applied as `capital *= (1 + ret * self.bet_size)`. So if ret is negative, capital decreases. If ret is positive, capital increases. That's typical for fixed fractional betting.\n\nNow, if we have 77% accuracy, meaning 77% of trades have ret positive? Actually accuracy is defined as winning trades count / total trades. In the simulation, winning means ret>0? Not exactly; the profit/loss depends on price direction and costs. If we predict UP and next_price > current_price, then long ret positive. {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_5a3f1693b8574af68c44b795","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→\"\"\"\n     2→Horizon ensemble: Integrate multi-horizon contextual signals.\n     3→\"\"\"\n     4→\n     5→import pandas as pd\n     6→import numpy as np\n     7→from typing import Dict, List\n     8→from ..models.population import Individual, Population\n     9→\n    10→\n    11→class HorizonEnsemble:\n    12→    \"\"\"\n    13→    Ensemble that uses multiple horizon-specific models as contextual features.\n    14→\n    15→    For each horizon (1h, 4h, 8h, 12h, 24h), we train a model on resampled data\n    16→    and use its prediction as a feature for the main 15m model.\n    17→    \"\"\"\n    18→\n    19→    def __init__(self, horizons: List[str] = None, population: Population = None):\n    20→        self.horizons = horizons or [\"1h\", \"4h\", \"8h\", \"12h\", \"24h\"]\n    21→        self.population = population\n    22→        self.horizon_models: Dict[str, Population] = {}  # horizon -> population\n    23→        self.main_features = []  # list of feature names produced by this ensemble\n    24→        self.is_fitted = False\n    25→\n    26→    def fit_horizon_models(\n    27→        self,\n    28→        horizon_data: Dict[str, pd.DataFrame],\n    29→        target_series: pd.Series\n    30→    ):\n    31→        \"\"\"\n    32→        Train a separate model population for each horizon.\n    33→\n    34→        Args:\n    35→            horizon_data: dict of {horizon: DataFrame} with features for that horizon\n    36→            target_series: target series (same for all, aligned to base timeframe)\n    37→        \"\"\"\n    38→        self.horizon_models = {}\n    39→\n    40→        horizons = list(horizon_data.items())\n    41→        print(f\"Fitting horizon ensembles for {len(horizons)} horizons\", flush=True)\n    42→        for idx, (horizon, df) in enumerate(horizons):\n    43→            if df.empty:\n    44→                print(f\"  Horizon {horizon}: empty, skipping\", flush=True)\n    45→                continue\n    46→\n    47→            print(f\"  Horizon {horizon} ({idx+1}/{len(horizons)}): processing {len(df)} rows\", flush=True)\n    48→            # Align target to this horizon's timestamps\n    49→            df_indexed = df.set_index(\"open_time\")\n    50→            target_aligned = target_series.reindex(df_indexed.index)\n    51→\n    52→            # Prepare features (exclude time and target columns)\n    53→            feature_cols = [c for c in df.columns if c not in [\"open_time\", \"open\", \"high\", \"low\", \"close\", \"volume\"]]\n    54→            X = df[feature_cols]\n    55→            print(f\"    Feature count: {len(feature_cols)}\", flush=True)\n    56→\n    57→            # Create population (use only random_forest for speed and stability)\n    58→            pop = Population(\n    59→                population_size=5,  # small for horizon models\n    60→                model_types=[\"random_forest\"],\n    61→                elitism_count=1\n    62→            )\n    63→            pop.initialize(input_dim=len(feature_cols), feature_names=feature_cols)\n    64→\n    65→            # Quick training: just fit all models (simplified, should be evolutionary)\n    66→            print(f\"    Training {len(pop.individuals)} models...\", flush=True)\n    67→            self._train_population(pop, X, target_aligned)\n    68→            self.horizon_models[horizon] = pop\n    69→            print(f\"    Completed horizon {horizon}\", flush=True)\n    70→\n    71→        self.is_fitted = True\n    72→        print(\"All horizon ensembles fitted\", flush=True)\n    73→\n    74→    def _train_population(self, population: Population, X: pd.DataFrame, y: pd.Series):\n    75→        \"\"\"Fit all models in a population.\"\"\"\n    76→        for ind in population.individuals:\n    77→            try:\n    78→                ind.model.fit(X, y)\n    79→            except Exception as e:\n    80→                # Set very low fitness if model fails\n    81→                ind.fitness = -1000.0\n    82→\n    83→    def generate_features(\n    84→        self,\n    85→        horizon_data: Dict[str, pd.DataFrame],\n    86→        base_index: pd.DatetimeIndex\n    87→    ) -> pd.DataFrame:\n    88→        \"\"\"\n    89→        Generate horizon ensemble features: predictions from each horizon's best model.\n    90→\n    91→        Returns:\n    92→            DataFrame with columns like: h_1h_pred, h_1h_proba, h_4h_pred, ...\n    93→        \"\"\"\n    94→        if not self.is_fitted:\n    95→            raise RuntimeError(\"HorizonEnsemble must be fitted first\")\n    96→\n    97→        features = pd.DataFrame(index=base_index)\n    98→\n    99→        for horizon, pop in self.horizon_models.items():\n   100→            best = pop.get_best(1)[0]\n   101→\n   102→            # horizon -> population\n        self.main_features = []  # list of feature names produced by this ensemble\n        self.is_fitted = False\n\n    def fit_horizon_models(\n        self,\n        horizon_data: Dict[str, pd.DataFrame],\n        target_series: pd.Series\n    ):\n        \"\"\"\n        Train a separate model population for each horizon.\n\n        Args:\n            horizon_data: dict of {horizon: DataFrame} with features for that horizon\n            target_series: target series (same for all, aligned to base timeframe)\n        \"\"\"\n        self.horizon_models = {}\n\n        horizons = list(horizon_data.items())\n        print(f\"Fitting horizon ensembles for {len(horizons)} horizons\", flush=True)\n        for idx, (horizon, df) in enumerate(horizons):\n            if df.empty:\n                print(f\"  Horizon {horizon}: empty, skipping\", flush=True)\n                continue\n\n            print(f\"  Horizon {horizon} ({idx+1}/{len(horizons)}): processing {len(df)} rows\", flush=True)\n            # Align target to this horizon's timestamps\n            df_indexed = df.set_index(\"open_time\")\n            target_aligned = target_series.reindex(df_indexed.index)\n\n            # Prepare features (exclude time and target columns)\n            feature_cols = [c for c in df.columns if c not in [\"open_time\", \"open\", \"high\", \"low\", \"close\", \"volume\"]]\n            X = df[feature_cols]\n            print(f\"    Feature count: {len(feature_cols)}\", flush=True)\n\n            # Create population (use only random_forest for speed and stability)\n            pop = Population(\n                population_size=5,  # small for horizon models\n                model_types=[\"random_forest\"],\n                elitism_count=1\n            )\n            pop.initialize(input_dim=len(feature_cols), feature_names=feature_cols)\n\n            # Quick training: just fit all models (simplified, should be evolutionary)\n            print(f\"    Training {len(pop.individuals)} models...\", flush=True)\n            self._train_population(pop, X, target_aligned)\n            self.horizon_models[horizon] = pop\n            print(f\"    Completed horizon {horizon}\", flush=True)\n\n        self.is_fitted = True\n        print(\"All horizon ensembles fitted\", flush=True)\n\n    def _train_population(self, population: Population, X: pd.DataFrame, y: pd.Series):\n        \"\"\"Fit all models in a population.\"\"\"\n        for ind in population.individuals:\n            try:\n                ind.model.fit(X, y)\n            except Exception as e:\n                # Set very low fitness if model fails\n                ind.fitness = -1000.0\n\n    def generate_features(\n        self,\n        horizon_data: Dict[str, pd.DataFrame],\n        base_index: pd.DatetimeIndex\n    ) -> pd.DataFrame:\n        \"\"\"\n        Generate horizon ensemble features: predictions from each horizon's best model.\n\n        Returns:\n            DataFrame with columns like: h_1h_pred, h_1h_proba, h_4h_pred, ...\n        \"\"\"\n        if not self.is_fitted:\n            raise RuntimeError(\"HorizonEnsemble must be fitted first\")\n\n        features = pd.DataFrame(index=base_index)\n\n        for horizon, pop in self.horizon_models.items():\n            best = pop.get_best(1)[0]\n\n            # Get horizon data aligned to base index\n            df_h = horizon_data[horizon].set_index(\"open_time\")\n            X_h = df_h.reindex(base_index)\n\n            # Drop non-feature columns\n            feature_cols = [c for c in X_h.columns if c not in [\"open\", \"high\", \"low\", \"close\", \"volume\", \"quote_volume\"]]\n            X_h_features = X_h[feature_cols]\n\n            # Forward fill any missing features (from resampling gaps)\n            X_h_features = X_h_features.ffill()\n\n            # Generate predictions\n            try:\n                proba = best.model.predict_proba(X_h_features)\n                pred = best.model.predict(X_h_features, threshold=0.5)\n                features[f\"h_{horizon}_proba\"] = proba\n                features[f\"h_{horizon}_pred\"] = pred\n            except Exception as e:\n                # If prediction fails, fill with 0.5 (neutral)\n                features[f\"h_{horizon}_proba\"] = 0.5\n                features[f\"h_{horizon}_pred\"] = 0\n\n        self.main_features = list(features.columns)\n        return features\n\n    def get_feature_names(self) -> List[str]:\n        return self.main_features\n","numLines":129,"startLine":1,"totalLines":129}}}
